merger_description:
MidOcean Energy proposes to indirectly acquire a:

* ~0.417% interest in the Gorgon LNG Project located on Barrow Island, Western Australia; and
* ~0.735% interest in the Ichthys LNG Project located in the Browse Basin in offshore Western Australia, including in the exploration activities of the expired WA-285-P tenement

via the acquisition by a newly incorporated subsidiary, MidOcean Australia Holdings 2 Pty Ltd of 100% of the shares in JERA Gorgon Pty Ltd (JERA Gorgon) and JERA Ichthys Pty Ltd from JERA Australia Pty Ltd, pursuant to a share sale agreement (the Acquisition).

The Acquisition has given rise to a pre-empt right in favour of the other joint venture participants to the Ichthys LNG Project. As a result, one of the Ichthys joint venture participants has exercised its right to acquire the underlying Ichthys LNG Project interests held indirectly by JERA Gorgon. This acquisition will occur following completion of the Acquisition.

MidOcean Energy is a liquefied natural gas (LNG) company that supplies natural gas in Australia and LNG and condensate overseas. In Australia, it holds minority interests in the Gorgon LNG Project and Pluto LNG Project in Western Australia and the QCLNG Project in Queensland.

JERA Gorgon supplies natural gas in Australia and LPG, LNG and condensate overseas, and its interests in Australia include the Gorgon LNG Project and the Ichthys LNG Project and its associated exploration permit, both located in WA.

The parties overlap in the production and supply of natural gas in Australia.

events.title: MidOcean Energy - JERA Gorgon - Phase 1 determination
events.display_title: MidOcean Energy - JERA Gorgon - Phase 1 determination
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Williams pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
MidOcean Energy proposes to indirectly acquire a:
• ~0.417% interest in the Gorgon LNG Project located on
Barrow Island, Western Australia, and
• ~0.735% interest in the Ichthys LNG Project located in the
Browse Basin in offshore Western Australia, including in the
exploration activities of the expired WA-285-P tenement
via the acquisition by a newly incorporated subsidiary, MidOcean
Australia Holdings 2 Pty Ltd (MidOcean Holdings) of 100% of the
shares in JERA Gorgon Pty Ltd (JERA Gorgon) from JERA Australia
Pty Ltd (JERA Australia), pursuant to a share sale agreement (the
Acquisition).
The Acquisition has given rise to a pre-empt right in favour of the
other joint venture participants to the Ichthys LNG Project. As a
result, one of the Ichthys joint venture participants has exercised its
right to acquire the underlying Ichthys LNG Project interests held
indirectly by JERA Gorgon. This subsequent acquisition would occur
following completion of the Acquisition and as such, has not been
considered as part of this determination.

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABZE(1) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition may be put into effect.

MidOcean Energy
The acquirer, MidOcean Holdings, is part of MidOcean Energy, which
is a liquefied natural gas (LNG) company that supplies natural gas in
Australia and LNG and condensate overseas. It holds minority
interests in LNG projects located in Western Australia (WA) and
Queensland, including a:
• 1% interest in the Gorgon LNG Project: a three train LNG
facility and domestic gas plant on Barrow Island, WA,
• 5% interest in the Pluto LNG Project: a one train LNG project
on the Burrup Peninsula, WA, and
• 1.25% interest in the QCLNG Project: a two-train onshore
coal seam gas project on Curtis Island, Queensland.
MidOcean Energy is managed by EIG Management Company, LLC
and its affiliates, a US institutional investor that specialises in
investments in energy and energy-related infrastructure globally.
JERA Gorgon
The target, JERA Gorgon, is a subsidiary of JERA Australia, which in
turn is owned by a Japanese-incorporated power generation
company. JERA Gorgon supplies natural gas in Australia and LNG,
liquefied petroleum gas and condensate overseas. Its interests in
Australia includes:
• a ~0.417% interest in the Gorgon LNG Project, and
• 100% of the shares in JERA Ichthys Pty Ltd, which in turn
holds ~0.735% interest in the Ichthys LNG Project and its
associated exploration permit. The Ichthys LNG Project
extracts gas in WA which is transported via pipeline to a
two-train LNG facility in the Northern Territory.

MidOcean Energy and JERA Gorgon (the Parties) horizontally
overlap in the production and supply of natural gas in WA.

When making a determination in Phase 1, the Australian Competition
and Consumer Commission (ACCC) undertakes a competition
assessment and considers whether it is appropriate for an
acquisition to be approved or subject to further assessment in
Phase 2 in accordance with section 51ABZJ of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act). In doing so, the ACCC must have
regard to the object of the Act and all relevant matters, including the
interests of consumers.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notified acquisitions, see the ACCC’s merger assessment guidelines
and interim merger process guidelines.
In conducting its competition assessment, the ACCC has considered
the information and documents that were submitted with the
notification form and publicly available information.
The ACCC has determined that the Acquisition may be put into
effect as it considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to have the
effect of substantially lessening competition in any market. In
reaching its decision, and based on the material before it, the ACCC
makes the following findings.
• The Acquisition would result in a very small change in MidOcean
Energy’s shareholding in the Gorgon LNG Project that would not
materially change MidOcean Energy’s influence or control over
the project.
• The Acquisition would provide MidOcean Energy with a very
small shareholding in the Ichthys LNG Project that would not
confer control or influence over the project.
• The Acquisition would lead to a very small increase in MidOcean
Energy’s share of gas production in WA. MidOcean Energy would
remain a very small supplier of domestic gas in WA and continue
to face competition from a range of other suppliers.

A notifying party, or other person who has been allowed to do so by
the Australian Competition Tribunal, may apply for review if they are
dissatisfied with the determination. Pursuant to section 100C of the
Act, applications for review of the determination are to be made to
the Australian Competition Tribunal before the end of 14 calendar
days after this statement of reasons was included on the ACCC’s
Acquisitions Register. To confirm whether there has been any
application for review, please contact the Australian Competition
Tribunal.

merger_name: Eagers Vic - Audi Melbourne and Audi Richmond
merger_description:
Eagers Vic Pty Ltd (**Eagers**) is a subsidiary of Eagers Automotive Limited (ASX: APE), a publicly listed automotive retail group which owns and operates motor vehicle dealerships across Australia. Eagers Automotive Limited sells new and used motor vehicles through its dealerships, representing around 50 automotive brands. Eagers also provides related goods and services including motor vehicle service, parts and allied consumer finance. Eagers operates 12 dealerships in the Melbourne area, including in Toorak (Mercedes-Benz), Malvern (ULR Jaguar and ULR Land Rover) and Port Melbourne (Jaguar, Volvo, Volkswagen and Land Rover). Eagers does not currently have any Audi dealerships in Victoria.

Zagame Automotive Group Pty Ltd (**Zagame**) operates motor vehicle dealerships (franchises and agencies), selling new and used motor vehicles, focusing on sports, luxury and performance vehicles. It represents 16 automotive brands with dealerships across the Melbourne area (including five Audi dealerships). Zagame also operates related businesses including automotive repairs, wholesale automotive parts, and retail automotive finance.

Eagers proposes to acquire the assets for the following Audi dealerships from Zagame:

Audi Centre Melbourne, 501 Swanston St, Melbourne 3000 (**Audi Melbourne**); and

Audi Richmond, 408 – 410 Swan Street, Richmond 3121 (**Audi Richmond**).

merger_description:
International Business Machines Corporation (**IBM**) proposes to acquire 100% of the shares in Confluent, Inc (**Confluent**).

IBM is a corporation headquartered in Armonk, New York, United States of America, and is publicly listed on the New York Stock Exchange (NYSE: IBM). It is active worldwide in the development, production and marketing of a wide variety of IT solutions, including enterprise IT software and systems (such as servers, storage systems, cloud, and cognitive offerings) and IT implementation services (such as business consulting and IT infrastructure services).

Confluent is headquartered in Mountain View, California, United States of America, and is publicly listed on the Nasdaq Global Select Market (Nasdaq: CFLT). Confluent is a data-streaming platform company built on Apache Kafka, which is an open-source technology. Confluent provides cloud-native and on-premise offerings that enable organisations to publish, subscribe to, store, and process real-time data streams at scale for use in building real-time applications and event-driven architectures.

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has

merger_name: Apex - Mercer Administration Services and related assets

anzsic_codes.name: Other Auxiliary Finance and Investment Services

merger_description:
Apex Group Limited (Apex), through its wholly owned subsidiary, Apex Group Holdings (Australia) Pty Ltd proposes to acquire all of the issued share capital in Mercer Administration Services (Australia) Pty Limited (Mercer Administration Services) and certain assets (including contracts and records necessary to service the contracts) of Mercer Outsourcing (Australia) Pty Ltd (Mercer Outsourcing) from Mercer (Australia) Pty Ltd and Mercer Outsourcing (the Acquisition).

Apex is a global financial solutions provider, offering a suite of services to superannuation funds, asset managers, capital markets, family offices and corporates. Apex provides a broad range of services including investor services, custody, private equity, corporate services, and fund administration.

Mercer Administration Services and Mercer Outsourcing (together, Mercer) are both providers of outsourcing and administration services, primarily for the Australian superannuation industry. They manage administrative functions for a wide range of superannuation funds.

Both Apex and Mercer supply administration services to superannuation funds in Australia. Administration services include fund administration (contribution and benefit payments, service improvement and development), fund accounting, insurance administration (including claims support) and risk administration (incident and complaints management, systems analysis and insurance remediation).

events.title: Questionnaire - Apex - Mercer
events.url_gh: /mergers/MN-10005/Questionnaire - Apex - Mercer.pdf

events.title: Apex - Mercer Administration Services and related assets - Phase 1 determination
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by a division of the Commission constituted by a direction issued pursuant to section 19 of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
Apex Group Limited (Apex), through its wholly owned subsidiary
Apex Group Holdings Australia Pty Ltd, proposes to acquire all of the
issued share capital in Mercer Administration Services (Australia)
Pty Limited (Mercer Administration Services) and certain assets
(including contracts and records necessary to service the contracts)
of Mercer Outsourcing (Australia) Pty Ltd (Mercer Outsourcing)
from Mercer (Australia) Pty Ltd and Mercer Outsourcing (the
Acquisition).

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABZE(1) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition may be put into effect.

The acquirer, Apex, is a global financial solutions provider that
supplies a range of services including investor services, custody,
private equity, corporate services, and fund administration. Its
customers include superannuation funds, asset managers, capital
markets, family offices and corporate clients.
The targets, Mercer Administration Services and Mercer Outsourcing
(together, Mercer) are both providers of outsourcing and
administration services, primarily for the Australian superannuation
industry. They manage administrative functions for a wide range of
superannuation funds.

determination_table_content.item:
Overlap and relationship
between the parties
determination_table_content.details:
In Australia, Apex and Mercer (together, the Parties) overlap in the
supply of administration services to superannuation funds and
trustees. These services include:
• fund administration (contribution and benefit payments,
service improvement and development)
• fund accounting
• insurance administration (including claims support), and
• risk administration (incident and complaints management,
systems analysis and insurance remediation).
Apex is also active in the supply of superannuation registry software
to customers including superannuation funds and superannuation
administration service providers. Superannuation registry software
can be used to manage member and employer information, process
transactions, and ensure regulatory compliance within the Australian
superannuation system.

Superannuation funds require a range of administration services to
operate on a day-to-day basis. These services can be performed in-
house by the fund itself, by a third party, or by adopting a hybrid
model whereby the fund is partly administered in-house and partly by
a third party.
Superannuation administration services include:
• management and distribution of benefit payments to
members
• data and document management
• member communication – production and issuing of
member and employer statements
• management of employer contributions and processing of
contributions into funds
• administering member investment choice
• customer contact services
• insurance and claims management
• online member and employer services
• financial and accounting services
• risk and compliance management
Customers of these services are typically trustees of funds,
including public sector, industry, retail and corporate funds.

When making a determination in Phase 1, the Australian Competition
and Consumer Commission (ACCC) undertakes a competition
assessment and considers whether it is appropriate for an
acquisition to be approved or subject to further assessment in
Phase 2 in accordance with section 51ABZJ of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act). In doing so, the ACCC must have
regard to the object of the Act and all relevant matters, including the
interests of consumers.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notified acquisitions, see the ACCC’s merger assessment guidelines
and interim merger process guidelines.
In conducting its competition assessment, the ACCC has considered
the information and documents that were submitted with the
notification form, information from third parties and publicly
available information.
The ACCC has determined that the Acquisition may be put into
effect as it considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to have the
effect of substantially lessening competition in any market. In
reaching its decision, and based on the material before it, the ACCC
makes the following findings:
• In relation to the supply of superannuation administration
services by third parties, while most market participants
expressed no concerns with the Acquisition, some noted that
there is limited competition or high concentration in the
superannuation administration industry, and that it has high
barriers to entry. The ACCC considers that:
o the Parties’ market share aggregation is estimated to be low,
and Apex would have a share of less than 15% following the
Acquisition
o the Parties have not competed closely in recent years, and
Mercer’s superannuation administration services business
has been in decline
o Apex would continue to face competition from numerous
alternative suppliers of superannuation administration
services in Australia (including MUFG/Link, FNZ, SS&C, Grow
and GBST).
• In relation to the supply of superannuation registry software,
Apex would be unlikely to have the ability or incentive to
foreclose rival superannuation administration service providers’
access to superannuation registry software, as Apex would
continue to face competition from alternative suppliers of
superannuation registry software in Australia, such as Bravura,
GBST and FNZ.
• Apex would be unlikely to have an ability to engage in anti-
competitive bundling or tying when seeking to sell
superannuation administration services and superannuation
registry software to superannuation fund customers, as it would
continue to face competition from alternative suppliers of both
superannuation administration services and superannuation
registry software.

A notifying party, or other person who has been allowed to do so by
the Australian Competition Tribunal, may apply for review if they are
dissatisfied with the determination. Pursuant to section 100C of the
Act, applications for review of the determination are to be made to
the Australian Competition Tribunal before the end of 14 calendar
days after this statement of reasons was included on the ACCC’s
Acquisitions Register. To confirm whether there has been any
application for review, please contact the Australian Competition
Tribunal.

merger_name: Australian Venue Co. – four licensed venues in metropolitan Sydney (Public House Petersham, The Erko, The Golden Sheaf and Barangaroo House)

targets.name: Sherilyn Pty. Limited as trustee for Solotrust Unit Trust

Newington Nominees Pty Limited as trustee for Newington Trust
Mash Barangaroo Pty. Limited as trustee for The Mash Barangaroo Trust
Ersko Pty Limited as trustee for The Ersko Trust

merger_description:
AVC Operations Pty Limited (**AVC**), a subsidiary of Australian Venue Co. Holdings Limited, proposes to acquire substantially all of the assets (including the exclusive right to carry on the businesses in succession of the vendors) of four venues in NSW pursuant to two interdependent transactions: (a) Three Asset Sale Agreement: acquiring Public House Petersham, The Erko, and The Golden Sheaf; and (b) Barangaroo Asset Sale Agreement: acquiring Barangaroo House.

AVC operates licensed venues throughout Australia and New Zealand, primarily pubs, as well as bars, clubs and licensed restaurants. AVC's venues are individually branded and are predominantly located in inner-city and metropolitan areas, offering a range of products and services including food, alcoholic and non-alcoholic beverages, function rooms, live entertainment and gaming facilities.

The four venues are: Public House Petersham: pub in Petersham; The Erko (Erskineville Hotel): hotel in Erskineville with food and accommodation; The Golden Sheaf: pub and late-night venue in Double Bay; and Barangaroo House: three-level dining venue in Barangaroo. Public House Petersham, The Erko, and The Golden Sheaf are owned by Solotel Group entities, which operates 27 licensed venues across Sydney, Parramatta, and Brisbane. Barangaroo House is co-owned by Solotel Group and Matt Moran.

events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by a division of the Commission constituted by a direction issued pursuant to section 19 of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
AVC Operations Pty Limited (AVC) proposes to acquire substantially
all of the assets (including the exclusive right to carry on the
businesses in succession of the vendors) of Public House
Petersham, The Erko, The Golden Sheaf and Barangaroo House (the
Target Venues) (together, the Acquisition).

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABZE(1) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition may be put into effect.

The acquirer, AVC, is a subsidiary of Australian Venue Co. Holdings
Limited, which operates licensed venues throughout Australia and
New Zealand, primarily pubs, as well as bars, clubs and licensed
restaurants. AVC’s venues are individually branded and
predominantly located in inner-city and metropolitan areas, offering
food, alcoholic and non-alcoholic beverages, function rooms, live
entertainment and gaming facilities.
Public House Petersham is a pub located at 292 Stanmore Road,
Petersham NSW 2049. The seller is Newington Nominees Pty
Limited as trustee for Newington Trust.
The Erko (Erskineville Hotel) is a hotel with food and
accommodation located at 102 Erskineville Road, Erskineville NSW
2043. The seller is Ersko Pty Limited as trustee for The Ersko Trust.
The Golden Sheaf is a pub and late-night venue located at 429 New
South Head Road, Double Bay NSW 2026. The seller is Sherilyn Pty.
Limited as trustee for Solotrust Unit Trust.
Barangaroo House is a three-level dining venue located at Building
R1, Southern Residue, 35 Barangaroo Avenue, Barangaroo NSW
2000. The seller is Barangaroo Pty Limited as trustee for Barangaroo
Trust and Mash Barangaroo Pty. Limited as trustee for The Mash
Barangaroo Trust.
Public House Petersham, The Erko, and the Golden Sheaf are owned
by Solotel Group entities, which operate 27 licensed venues across
Sydney, Paramatta, and Brisbane. Barangaroo House is co-owned by
Solotel Group and Matt Moran.

The parties overlap in the supply of retail hospitality venues in
metropolitan Sydney.

When making a determination in Phase 1, the Australian Competition
and Consumer Commission (ACCC) undertakes a competition
assessment and considers whether it is appropriate for an
acquisition to be approved or subject to further assessment in
Phase 2 in accordance with section 51ABZJ of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act). In doing so, the ACCC must have
regard to the object of the Act and all relevant matters, including the
interests of consumers.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notified acquisitions, see the ACCC’s merger assessment guidelines
and interim merger process guidelines.
In conducting its competition assessment, the ACCC has considered
the information and documents that were submitted with the
notification form and publicly available information.
The ACCC has determined that the Acquisition may be put into
effect as it considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to have the
effect of substantially lessening competition in any relevant market.
In reaching its decision, and based on the material before it, the
ACCC makes the following findings.
• The aggregation in retail hospitality venues arising from the
Acquisition is minimal in respect of each Target Venue including
at a local level.
• AVC will continue to face competition from alternative retail
hospitality venues in the local areas surrounding the Target
Venues.

A notifying party, or other person who has been allowed to do so by
the Australian Competition Tribunal, may apply for review if they are
dissatisfied with the determination. Pursuant to section 100C of the
Act, applications for review of the determination are to be made to
the Australian Competition Tribunal before the end of 14 calendar
days after this statement of reasons was included on the ACCC’s
Acquisitions Register. To confirm whether there has been any
application for review, please contact the Australian Competition
Tribunal.

merger_description:
Google LLC (**Google**) proposes to acquire 100% of the share capital in Wiz Inc (**Wiz**).

Google supplies a wide range of digital products and services in Australia, including general search services, a mobile operating system (Android), a mobile app marketplace (the Play Store) and advertising technology services, and operates across multiple layers of the generative AI stack. Google supplies a range of cloud computing services to Australian consumers and businesses through its Google Cloud division, and also offers several cloud security products.

Wiz offers cloud security services to enable organisations to identify and prevent critical security risks across multiple cloud platforms, as well as in hybrid and on-premises environments. Wiz’s primary product is a cloud native application protection platform (**CNAPP**) called Wiz Cloud. Wiz also offers add-ons to Wiz Cloud to enhance its capabilities, including Wiz Sensor, Wiz Defend and Wiz Code.

events.display_title: Questionnaire released for Google acquisition of Wiz

determination_table_content.details:
Google LLC (Google)’s proposed acquisition of 100% of the share
capital in Wiz, Inc. (Wiz) (the Acquisition).

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABZE(1) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition may be put into effect.

The acquirer, Google, is a large, multinational technology company
headquartered in the United States. Google supplies a wide range of
digital products and services in Australia, including cloud
infrastructure through the Google Cloud Platform (Google Cloud).
Google Cloud’s products include the following cloud security
products:
• Security Operations (SecOps)
• Threat Intelligence
• Mandiant Managed Defense, Response & Consulting
(Mandiant)
• Security Command Center (SCC) and Security Command
Center Enterprise (SCCE).
Google’s generative AI foundation models (Gemini) can be
integrated into cloud security products including cloud-native
application protection platforms (CNAPPs).
The target, Wiz, is a private cloud security firm headquartered in the
United States. Wiz offers cloud security solutions that enable
organisations to identify and prevent security risks across multiple
cloud platforms, as well as hybrid and on-premises environments.
Wiz’s key service is Wiz Cloud. It offers several add-ons to Wiz Cloud
(Wiz Sensor, Wiz Defend and Wiz Code), and offers a commercial
package of all of its products together which it markets as Wiz
CNAPP.
The reasons for the ACCC’s determination are set out in section 2.

merger_description:
Cvent Atlanta, LLC (Cvent) proposes to acquire ON24, Inc. (ON24), through the merger of Summit Sub Corp. (an indirect wholly owned subsidiary of Cvent) with ON24. Following the merger, ON24 will be the surviving entity and continue as an indirect wholly owned subsidiary of Cvent (Proposed Acquisition).

Cvent provides meetings, events and hospitality technology, specifically an event marketing and management platform. Cvent is solely controlled by investment funds managed by affiliates of Blackstone, Inc.

In Australia, Cvent provides cloud-based services for hospitality and event management, and limited webinar services.

ON24 is a cloud-based engagement platform that provides tools for webinars, virtual events and other experiences. It also provides marketing and sales analytics tools.

In Australia, ON24 supplies webinar services and to a lesser extent virtual event services software.

events.title: Cvent - ON24 - Phase 1 Determination
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by a division of the Commission constituted by a direction issued pursuant to section 19 of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
Cvent Atlanta, LLC (Cvent)’s proposed acquisition of ON24, Inc.
(ON24), through the merger of Summit Sub Corp. (an indirect wholly
owned subsidiary of Cvent) with ON24. Following the merger, ON24
will be the surviving entity and continue as an indirect wholly owned
subsidiary of Cvent (the Acquisition).

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABZE(1) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition may be put into effect.

The acquirer, Cvent,provides meetings, events and hospitality
technology, specifically an event marketing and management
platform. Cvent is solely controlled by investment funds managed by
affiliates of Blackstone, Inc.
Blackstone is one of the largest alternative asset managers
operating globally.
The target, ON24, is a cloud-based engagement platform that
provides tools for webinars, virtual events and other experiences. It
also provides marketing and sales analytics tools.

Cvent and ON24 (the Parties) both supply software related to
meetings and events.
Cvent’s event management tool, AttendeeHub, enables businesses
to host in-person events such as conferences. Its services include
venue and attendee management, customised event websites and
apps, and data analytics. It can also be used for virtual events and
webinars. The ACCC understands that AttendeeHub is marketed
primarily to event management professionals.
ON24’s tool, Webcast Elite, delivers live and on-demand webinar
services. ON24 also has a hybrid event platform which manages
participant engagement across in-person and online attendees. The
ACCC understands that Webcast Elite is marketed primarily to
marketing professionals.

When making a determination in Phase 1, the Australian Competition
and Consumer Commission (ACCC) undertakes a competition
assessment and considers whether it is appropriate for an
acquisition to be approved or subject to further assessment in
Phase 2 in accordance with section 51ABZJ of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act). In doing so, the ACCC must have
regard to the object of the Act and all relevant matters, including the
interests of consumers.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notified acquisitions, see the ACCC’s merger assessment guidelines
and interim merger process guidelines.
In conducting its competition assessment, the ACCC has considered
the information and documents that were submitted with the
notification form and publicly available information.
The ACCC has determined that the Acquisition may be put into
effect as it considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to have the
effect of substantially lessening competition in any market. In
reaching its decision, and based on the material before it, the ACCC
makes the following findings.
• The Parties are not close competitors to each other with their
services performing distinct functions. ON24 does not offer
services which are substitutable for Cvent’s in-person event
management platform. Cvent provides a webinar service but has
very little revenue in Australia.
• Post acquisition, Cvent will continue to face competition from
alternative suppliers of webinar services and alternative
suppliers of event services in Australia, including Zoom, Teams,
Webex, EventsAir and GoTo.

A notifying party, or other person who has been allowed to do so by
the Australian Competition Tribunal, may apply for review if they are
dissatisfied with the determination. Pursuant to section 100C of the
Act, applications for review of the determination are to be made to
the Australian Competition Tribunal before the end of 14 calendar
days after this statement of reasons was included on the ACCC’s
Acquisitions Register. To confirm whether there has been any
application for review, please contact the Australian Competition
Tribunal.

Experience Australia Group Pty Ltd (EAG), via its wholly owned subsidiary JB Kakadu Hotel Pty Ltd (JB Kakadu), proposes to acquire Crocodile Hotel Pty Ltd, which operates the business and business assets known as the Kakadu Crocodile Hotel (Crocodile Hotel) in Jabiru (NT), from Kakadu Tourism (GCH) Pty Limited (the Seller), as well as a 100% leasehold interest in the land on which the Crocodile Hotel is situated from Gundjeihmi Aboriginal Corporation Jabiru Town (the Acquisition).

EAG primarily supplies tourism services and multi-destination packaged tours in Australia under the brand name “Journey Beyond”. Via its wholly owned subsidiary Outback Spirit Tours Pty Ltd (Outback Spirit), EAG also supplies touring packages to remote destinations across Western Australia, the Northern Territory, Queensland and South Australia.

The Crocodile Hotel is a 110‑room hotel located in the township of Jabiru in Kakadu National Park. In addition to accommodation, its facilities include a restaurant, bar and an on‑site art gallery featuring local artwork.

EAG and the Crocodile Hotel both operate in the touring services supply chain. Outback Spirit currently uses accommodation provided by the Crocodile Hotel on certain tours.

merger_description:
Rocket Software, Inc.(**Rocket Software**), a portfolio company of Bain Capital, proposes to acquire the Vertica Analytics Database Platform (**Vertica**) from Open Text Corporation (**Open Text**). The proposed acquisition will occur largely by way of asset sale.

Rocket Software is a global provider of infrastructure and application software solutions. Rocket’s products and services focus on modernising and optimising applications on mainframe computers. Bain Capital is a private equity investment firm that invests in companies across a number of industries, including information technology, healthcare, retail and consumer products, communications, financial services and industrial/manufacturing.

Vertica is an enterprise-grade analytical database software that currently forms part of Open Text. Vertica is designed for large-scale data analytics within a customer’s own IT environment.

events.title: Questionnaire - Rocket Software - Vertica
events.url_gh: /mergers/MN-20009/Rocket-Vertica - third-party questionnaire - March 2026.pdf

events.title: Rocket Vertica - Phase 1 determination
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Williams pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
Rocket Software, Inc. (Rocket Software)’s proposed acquisition of
the Vertica Analytics Database Platform (Vertica) from Open Text
Corporation (Open Text) (the Acquisition). The Acquisition will occur
largely by way of asset sale.

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABZE(1) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition may be put into effect.

The acquirer, Rocket Software, is a portfolio company of Bain
Capital. Rocket Software is a global provider of infrastructure and
application software solutions. Rocket Software’s products and
services focus on modernising and optimising applications on
mainframe computers. Bain Capital is a private equity investment
firm that invests in companies across a number of industries,
including information technology, healthcare, retail and consumer
products, communications, financial services and
industrial/manufacturing.
The target, Vertica, is an enterprise-grade analytical database
software that currently forms part of Open Text. Vertica is designed
for large-scale data analytics within a customer’s own IT
environment.

determination_table_content.item:
Overlap and relationship
between the parties
determination_table_content.details:
While Rocket Software and Vertica (together, the Parties) both
supply business analytics software, there is limited functional
overlap between their products. In particular, unlike Vertica, neither
Rocket Software, nor any other connected entity of Rocket Software
provides a dedicated platform for large-scale data analytics
workloads.
The ACCC understands that post acquisition, Vertica’s capabilities
could be used to improve certain aspects of Rocket Software’s
offerings with data analytics-related functionality. However, the
ACCC has not identified any other actual vertical or conglomerate
relationships between Vertica and Rocket Software or any other Bain
Capital portfolio company (or other connected entity of Rocket
Software) active in the technology sector. However, the Acquisition
could potentially give rise to a vertical relationship in future if one of
Rocket’s connected entities (including a company managed by Bain
Capital) were to become a supplier or customer of the Vertica
product.

When making a determination in Phase 1, the Australian Competition
and Consumer Commission (ACCC) undertakes a competition
assessment and considers whether it is appropriate for an
acquisition to be approved or subject to further assessment in
Phase 2 in accordance with section 51ABZJ of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act). In doing so, the ACCC must have
regard to the object of the Act and all relevant matters, including the
interests of consumers.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notified acquisitions, see the ACCC’s merger assessment guidelines
and interim merger process guidelines.
In conducting its competition assessment, the ACCC has considered
the information and documents that were submitted with the
notification form and publicly available information.
The ACCC has determined that the Acquisition may be put into
effect as it considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to have the
effect of substantially lessening competition in any market. In
reaching its decision, and based on the material before it, the ACCC
makes the following findings.
• There is no (or very limited) horizontal overlap between the
product offerings of Rocket Software and Vertica (including the
product offerings of the parties’ connected entities), given
differences in functionality.
• Post acquisition, Rocket Software would be unlikely to have an
ability and incentive to foreclose rivals’ access to a large-scale
data analytics platform, including by adopting an anti-
competitive bundling or tying strategy, given the availability of
multiple alternative suppliers of analytical database platforms,
including Microsoft, Oracle and Databricks.

A notifying party, or other person who has been allowed to do so by
the Australian Competition Tribunal, may apply for review if they are
dissatisfied with the determination. Pursuant to section 100C of the
Act, applications for review of the determination are to be made to
the Australian Competition Tribunal before the end of 14 calendar
days after this statement of reasons was included on the ACCC’s
Acquisitions Register. To confirm whether there has been any
application for review, please contact the Australian Competition
Tribunal.

merger_description:
Origin Energy International Holdings Pty Ltd (a wholly owned subsidiary of Origin Energy Limited) (**Origin**) is participating in a series of related transactions which are being effected to achieve the spin off of the Kraken technology business, operated by Kraken Technologies Limited and its related companies (**Kraken**), from Octopus Energy Group Limited (**OEGL**).

Origin Energy Limited is an ASX-listed integrated energy company which, relevantly in this context, engages in the retail supply of electricity and gas as well as broadband services in Australia. Origin has an existing interest in OEGL. OEGL, together with its related bodies corporate (**Octopus Energy**), is a UK‑based global energy retailer and energy services provider. While it operates retail businesses internationally, Octopus Energy is not a direct retail supplier in Australia.

Kraken is a technology provider, wholly owned by OEGL, that develops and licenses a proprietary retail and operations platform, called “the Kraken platform”. The platform is used by utility providers globally to manage billing, customer service, distributed energy resources and operational processes.

The acquisition is one of a series of transactions being entered into to enable the demerger to take place and Kraken to raise equity from new investors. In substance, the acquisition:

* maintains the economic interest which Origin currently holds in Kraken and OEGL; and
* removes and irrevocably waives the exclusivity which Origin previously held in respect of the Kraken platform in Australia.

Following the demerger of Kraken from Octopus Energy (under which existing OEGL shareholders will receive direct shares in Kraken Technologies Global Group Limited (**DemergerCo**)), the following transactions will be implemented in respect of Kraken:

* In connection with the demerger of Kraken, US$1 billion in equity will be raised in Kraken’s first standalone raising, supporting the separation from Octopus Energy (targeting first half of 2026);
* in order to maintain its existing economic interest, Origin will:
  
  + acquire approximately US$140 million (~AUD$210 million equivalent) in Kraken shares from another existing shareholder (CPP Investment Board Private Holdings (4) Inc.); and
  + be issued with an additional 1.5% equity interest in Kraken in exchange for agreeing to delete and remove Origin’s exclusivity to the Kraken platform in Australia.

merger_description:
Eli Lilly and Company (**Eli Lilly**) proposes to acquire 100% of the issued shares in Orna Therapeutics Holdings, LLC (**Orna Therapeutics**) (the **Acquisition**).

Eli Lilly is a US-based pharmaceutical company, which discovers, develops, manufactures and markets human pharmaceutical products for various therapeutic areas, including diabetes, oncology, immunology and neuroscience. Eli Lilly is headquartered in Indianapolis, US and is publicly listed on the New York Stock Exchange. Eli Lilly has an Australian subsidiary that registers and markets medicine in Australia.

Orna Therapeutics is a US-based pre-clinical biotechnology company developing a new class of engineered circular RNA (**oRNA**) therapeutics and immune tropic lipid nanoparticles (**LNP**) delivery technologies for the treatment of autoimmune disorders, and cancer conditions. Orna Therapeutics has a pipeline consisting of two pre-clinical in vivo Chimeric Antigen Receptor T-cell (**CAR-T**) therapies, designed to treat B cell-driven autoimmune diseases.

merger_description:
Symal Group Limited (Symal Group), through its wholly owned subsidiaries, proposes to acquire all of the business and assets of:

* the Timms business from Timms Contracting Pty Ltd, Timms Hire Pty Ltd and Teagun Investments Pty Ltd (collectively referred to as Timms Group); and
* the L&D business from Churinga (Investments) Pty Ltd as trustee for the Churinga Trust and Adawin Pty Ltd (collectively referred to as L&D Group).

As part of the transaction, Symal NG Property Pty Ltd may enter into a licence for the property located at 36 Frederick Street, Northgate, QLD 4013 (the Property), which is owned by Clarkson-Jones Investments (Qld) Pty Ltd, K Parsons Investments (Qld) Pty Ltd, Timms Investments (Qld) Pty Ltd and Jones Investments (Qld) Pty Ltd.

Symal Group is an Australian based self-performing contractor providing construction and engineering services, specialising in the roads, rail, defence, justice, ports and energy sectors. The services provided by Symal Group include various forms of construction, project management, plant and equipment hire, material sales and recycling and repurposing.

Timms Group is a Tier 2 contractor that provides plant-intensive civil works and hauling services for infrastructure and construction projects in Queensland. L&D Group is a Tier 1 contractor on infrastructure and construction projects in Queensland, including the provision of demolition, bulk earthworks, site preparation and construction services.

Symal Group does not currently supply the services offered by Timms Group and L&D Group in Queensland.

determination_table_content.details:
Symal Group Limited through the following wholly owned
subsidiaries:
• Symal Management Pty Ltd
• Symal Contractors (QLD) Pty Ltd
• Syl TM Contracting Pty Ltd
• T Haulage Pty Ltd
• T Operations Pty Ltd
• Symal NG Property Pty Ltd
(collectively referred to as Symal Group),
proposes to acquire all of the business and assets of the:
• Timms business from Timms Contracting Pty Ltd, Timms
Hire Pty Ltd and Teagun Investments Pty Ltd (collectively
referred to as Timms Group) and
• L&D business from Churinga (Investments) Pty Ltd as
trustee for the Churinga Trust and Adawin Pty Ltd
(collectively referred to as L&D Group).
As part of the transaction, Symal NG Property Pty Ltd may enter into
a licence for the property located at 36 Frederick Street, Northgate,
QLD 4013 (the Property) which is owned by Clarkson-Jones
Investments (Qld) Pty Ltd, K Parsons Investments (Qld) Pty Ltd,
Timms Investments (Qld) Pty Ltd and Jones Investments (Qld) Pty
Ltd.
Symal Group, Timms Group, and L&D Group are together referred to
as the Parties.

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABZE(1) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition may be put into effect.

Symal Group Limited is an Australian based self-performing
contractor providing construction and engineering services,
specialising in the roads, rail, defence, justice, ports and energy
sectors. The services provided by Symal Group Limited and its
subsidiaries include various forms of construction, project
management, plant and equipment hire, material sales and recycling
and repurposing.
The Timms Group is a contractor that provides plant-intensive civil
works and hauling services for infrastructure and construction
projects in Queensland.
The L&D Group is a contractor on infrastructure and construction
projects in Queensland, including the provision of demolition, bulk
earthworks, site preparation and construction services.

There is no direct overlap between the Parties. While the Parties
each supply civil contracting and construction services, Timms
Group and L&D Group supply civil contracting and construction
services in Queensland only, where Symal Group does not have a
direct presence in relation to the supply of these services.

When making a determination in Phase 1, the Australian Competition
and Consumer Commission (ACCC) undertakes a competition
assessment and considers whether it is appropriate for an
acquisition to be approved or subject to further assessment in
Phase 2 in accordance with section 51ABZJ of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act). In doing so, the ACCC must have
regard to the object of the Act and all relevant matters, including the
interests of consumers.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notified acquisitions, see the ACCC’s merger assessment guidelines
and interim merger process guidelines.
In conducting its competition assessment, the ACCC has considered
the information and documents that were submitted with the
notification form as well as further information provided by the
Parties following pre-notification engagement with the ACCC.
The ACCC has determined that the Acquisition may be put into
effect as it considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to have the
effect of substantially lessening competition in any market. In
reaching its decision, and based on the material before it, the ACCC
makes the following findings:
• The Parties would continue to face competition from
alternative suppliers of civil contracting and construction
haulage services in Queensland, including several
competitors which are of a similar or larger size to the
Parties.
• The Parties are not close competitors, as the Timms Group
and L&D Group focus on supplying civil contracting and
construction services within Brisbane, Queensland. Symal
Group does not have any direct presence in relation to these
services in Queensland.

A notifying party, or other person who has been allowed to do so by
the Australian Competition Tribunal, may apply for review if they are
dissatisfied with the determination. Pursuant to section 100C of the
Act, applications for review of the determination are to be made to
the Australian Competition Tribunal before the end of 14 calendar
days after this statement of reasons was included on the ACCC’s
Acquisitions Register. To confirm whether there has been any
application for review, please contact the Australian Competition
Tribunal.

merger_description:
ServiceNow, Inc. (**ServiceNow**) proposes to acquire Armis Security, Ltd (**Armis**).

ServiceNow is a US-based software company. ServiceNow primarily supplies enterprise software which brings together data, artificial intelligence (**AI**), company processes, assets and security into a single platform that employees can use for a range of purposes. ServiceNow’s products facilitate organisation workflows by turning fragmented operations into coordinated, autonomous workflows. These types of software products are known as ‘IT operations management’ or ITOM products. ServiceNow also supplies Operational Technology (**OT**) operations management (**OTOM**) products, which improve visibility, health, and serviceability of industrial systems in OT environments, including by enabling incident response and operational resilience. ‘OT’ describes technology that interacts directly with physical equipment like utility grids, industrial machinery or healthcare equipment.

Armis is a US-based software company specialising in cyber exposure management and security. Armis’ products help organisations see, protect and manage all points through which an unauthorised user could access a system and extract data. Armis’ products work across cyber-physical system environments, primarily focused on OT security. Armis provides a range of security products under its Armis Centrix platform, including products for Asset Management and Security, OT/IoT (Internet of Things) Security (SaaS and On-Premise), Secure Remote Access, Medical Device Security and Early Warning.

Both ServiceNow and Armis supply products that have:

* continuous threat exposure management (**CTEM**) capabilities for IT environments, and
* OT security solution capabilities.

events.title: ServiceNow - Armis - Phase 1 determination
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by a division of the Commission constituted by a direction issued pursuant to section 19 of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
ServiceNow, Inc. (ServiceNow) proposes to acquire 100% of the
issued shares in Armis Security, Ltd (Armis) (the Acquisition).

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABZE(1) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition may be put into effect.

The acquirer, ServiceNow, is a US-based software company.
ServiceNow primarily supplies enterprise software which brings
together data, artificial intelligence (AI), company processes, assets
and security into a single platform that employees can use for a
range of purposes. ServiceNow’s products facilitate organisation
workflows by turning fragmented operations into coordinated,
autonomous workflows.
The target, Armis, is a US-based software company specialising in
cyber exposure management and security. Armis’ products help
organisations see, protect and manage all points through which an
unauthorised user could access a system and extract data.
Armis’ products work across so-called ‘cyber-physical system’
environments, primarily focused on operational technology (OT)
security. OT describes technology that interacts directly with
physical equipment like utility grids, industrial machinery and
healthcare equipment. Armis provides a range of security products
under its Armis Centrix platform, including products for:
- asset management and security,
- OT/IoT (Internet of Things) security (SaaS and on-premise),
- secure remote access,
- medical device security, and
- early warning.

determination_table_content.item:
Overlap and relationship
between the parties
determination_table_content.details:
The parties each supply enterprise software products in Australia.
They overlap in the supply of:
• continuous threat exposure management (CTEM) software
products for IT environments, and
• OT security software products.
ServiceNow also supplies:
• IT operations management (ITOM) software, which is used
by companies to oversee and manage computing,
networking and application resources. CTEM software
products are often integrated and interoperable with ITOM
software.
• OT operations management (OTOM) software, which
enhances visibility, health, and serviceability of industrial
systems in OT environments, including by enabling rapid
incident response and operational resilience. OT security
software products are often integrated and interoperable
with OTOM software.
Armis’ CTEM products can be integrated with ServiceNow’s ITOM
products, as well as the products of other ITOM providers who
compete with ServiceNow. ServiceNow’s ITOM products permit
integrations with products supplied by many other CTEM providers.
Armis’ OT security products can be integrated with ServiceNow’s
OTOM products, as well as those of other OTOM providers who
compete with ServiceNow. ServiceNow’s OTOM products permit
integrations with products supplied by other OT security providers.

When making a determination in Phase 1, the ACCC undertakes a
competition assessment and considers whether it is appropriate for
an acquisition to be approved or subject to further assessment in
Phase 2 in accordance with section 51ABZJ of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act). In doing so, the ACCC must have
regard to the object of the Act and all relevant matters, including the
interests of consumers.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notified acquisitions, see the ACCC’s merger assessment guidelines
and interim merger process guidelines.
In conducting its competition assessment, the ACCC has considered
the information and documents that were submitted with the
notification form as well as further information provided by the
parties, publicly available information and feedback from market
participants.
The ACCC has determined that the Acquisition may be put into
effect as it considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to have the
effect of substantially lessening competition in any market in
Australia. In reaching its decision, and based on the material before
it, the ACCC makes the following findings.
• The parties are not close competitors in the supply of CTEM
software or OT security software.
• Post acquisition, the combined entity would continue to face
competition in the supply of both OT security software and
CTEM software from a number of alternative providers.
• ServiceNow would be unlikely to have the ability and
incentive to foreclose Armis’ competitors through, for
example, limiting the interoperability of its ITOM platform
with Armis’ competitors’ products. This is principally
because such a foreclosure strategy is likely to damage
ServiceNow’s reputation and customer relationships, and
not be profitable. ServiceNow’s key value proposition to its
customers is in providing an agnostic, interoperable
platform for an organisation’s workflows which affords
significant choice to its customers. Market feedback
confirmed this is valued highly. Information provided by the
merger parties, and confirmed by market inquiries, also
indicate that ServiceNow’s APIs are built on universally
accessible protocols.
• The merged entity would be unlikely to have the ability and
incentive to foreclose ServiceNow’s competitors that use
Armis’ products, for example, through restricting the
interoperability of Armis’ products with ServiceNow’s rivals.
There are a range of CTEM and OT security alternatives to
Armis’ products in Australia. Customers would be able to
switch to such products should ServiceNow attempt to limit
Armis’ interoperability with ServiceNow’s rivals.

A notifying party, or other person who has been allowed to do so by
the Australian Competition Tribunal, may apply for review if they are
dissatisfied with the determination. Pursuant to section 100C of the
Act, applications for review of the determination are to be made to
the Australian Competition Tribunal before the end of 14 calendar
days after this statement of reasons was included on the ACCC’s
Acquisitions Register. To confirm whether there has been any
application for review, please contact the Australian Competition
Tribunal.

merger_description:
KPS Special Situations Mid‑Cap Fund II (KPS) proposes to acquire 100% of the shares of Chargeurs Films de Protection SAS and its US subsidiaries (Novacel, Inc., Walco, Inc. and Novacel Performance Coatings, Inc.) (Novacel). Following completion, Compagnie Chargeurs Invest SA (the Seller), will roll-over an interest of approximately 25% in Novacel, with no joint control rights (the Acquisition).

KPS and its associated entities is a private investment company incorporated in the US. Through its funds, KPS makes equity investments in manufacturing and industrial companies across a diverse array of industries, including basic materials, branded consumer, healthcare and luxury products, automotive parts, capital equipment and general manufacturing.

Novacel is a global producer of specialty processing films used for protecting surfaces during manufacturing, assembly, transport or installation, as well as specialty tapes and kraft papers used to protect, hold, splice, wrap or strap industrial materials, and associated specialty machines used in industrial material handling and processing. Novacel only supplies processing and protective films in Australia.

Peter Warren Automotive Holdings Ltd (Peter Warren) proposes to acquire:

* 100% of the shares of Paul Wakeling Motor Group Pty Ltd;
* 100% of the shares of Wollongong City Motors Pty Ltd; and
* the business and trading net assets of Hurrikayne Pty Ltd, relating to the Moss Vale Motor Group

from Wakeling Automotive Group (Wakeling Automotive) (the Acquisition), as part of which Peter Warren will acquire 30 new and used car dealerships in New South Wales.

Peter Warren is an ASX-listed new and used car retailing business that operates over 80 automotive dealership sites at 25 locations across the east coast of Australia. Peter Warren represents 30 Original Equipment Manufacturer (OEM) vehicle brands as an authorised dealer, and also supplies OEM spare parts and aftermarket vehicle servicing.

Wakeling Automotive is a new and used car retailing business that operates 30 automotive dealership sites at 8 locations across Sydney, Wollongong and the Southern Highlands. Wakeling Automotive represents 16 OEM vehicle brands as an authorised dealer, and also supplies OEM spare parts and aftermarket vehicle servicing.

Both of Peter Warren and Wakeling Automotive supply new and used cars, OEM spare parts and aftermarket vehicle servicing in the Campbelltown/Narellan/Smeaton Grange suburbs of southern Sydney.

merger_description:
Acquisition

This notification concerns the proposed acquisition of certain Australian assets of Konvoy Holdings Pty Limited (Receivers and Managers Appointed) (Subject to Deed of Company Arrangement) (**Konvoy**) by Kegstar Pty Limited (**Kegstar**), a wholly owned subsidiary of MicroStar Logistics, LLC (**Acquisition**).

The Australian assets which are proposed to be acquired pursuant to the Acquisition include:

(a) kegs;

(b) keg tracking technology hardware and software (known as 'Katch');

(c) select employees; and

(d) a keg repair and maintenance facility.

The Parties

MicroStar Logistics, LLC, is a limited liability company incorporated in the United States and is indirectly wholly owned by MStar Holding Corporation. MicroStar entered the Australian market in 2021 via the acquisition of Kegstar from Brambles.

In Australia, Kegstar primarily offers a pay-per-fill keg management service (PPF). It also offers longer-term keg leasing.

Konvoy operates a keg services business in Australia and New Zealand. It offers short-term keg leasing (on a PPF basis), longer-term keg leasing, and keg maintenance and repair services.

Summary of horizontal and vertical overlaps

The Parties are both suppliers of PPF services and, to a much lesser extent, longer term keg leasing to brewery customers in Australia.

There is no vertical overlap between the parties.

merger_description:
Adobe Inc. (**Adobe**) through a newly incorporated direct wholly owned subsidiary, Fenway Merger Sub, Inc. (**Merger Sub**), proposes to acquire 100% of the share capital in Semrush Holdings, Inc. (Semrush and together with Adobe, the **Parties**). The Proposed Transaction is being implemented pursuant to a merger agreement entered into between Adobe, Merger Sub, and Semrush dated 18 November 2025 (**Merger Agreement**). Pursuant to the Merger Agreement, Merger Sub will merge with and into Semrush, such that Merger Sub will cease to exist and Semrush will become the "surviving corporation" and a direct, wholly owned subsidiary of Adobe. After the Proposed Transaction, Semrush will be solely controlled by Adobe.

Adobe is a global technology company. Adobe's main offerings can be divided between its Customer Experience Orchestration business (formerly known as the Digital Experience business), which focuses on accelerating customer experience and delivering business intelligence services, and its Creativity & Productivity business (formerly known as the Digital Media business), which focuses on helping users create, design, and publish rich content and 3D experiences, and improve how they view, share, and collaborate on documents and content. Adobe is headquartered in California and incorporated in Delaware, USA.

Semrush provides an online visibility management and content marketing service offering a range of tools to manage web brand presence, including through its search engine optimisation (**SEO**) toolkit. Semrush is headquartered in Massachusetts and incorporated in Delaware, USA.

The parties are both active in the supply of software for enterprise customers and overlap in relation to generative engine optimisation (**GEO**), also referred to as Answer Engine Optimisation or Artificial Intelligence Optimisation.

Semrush is predominantly an SEO provider and is also active - to a lesser extent - in the supply of GEO services through its AI Visibility Toolkit and Enterprise AI Optimization products (launched in March and June 2025 respectively). Adobe is not an SEO provider and started offering GEO services through its Large Language Model Optimiser in October 2025.

content marketing service offering a range of tools to manage web
brand presence, including through its search engine optimisation
toolkit. Semrush is headquartered in Massachusetts and
incorporated in Delaware, USA.

Adobe and Semrush (together, the Parties) overlap in the supply of
generative engine optimisation services in Australia. Generative
engine optimisation refers to the process of adapting a website so
that large language model platforms, such as ChatGPT, Gemini, and
Claude, can understand, extract, and cite it when answering user
queries.
Adobe supplies generative engine optimisation services through its
Large Language Model Optimiser product. Semrush supplies
generative engine optimisation services through its AI Visibility
Toolkit and Enterprise AI Optimization products.

When making a determination in Phase 1, the Australian Competition
and Consumer Commission (ACCC) undertakes a competition
assessment and considers whether it is appropriate for an
acquisition to be approved or subject to further assessment in
Phase 2 in accordance with section 51ABZJ of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act). In doing so, the ACCC must have
regard to the object of the Act and all relevant matters, including the
interests of consumers.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notified acquisitions, see the ACCC’s merger assessment guidelines
and interim merger process guidelines.
In conducting its competition assessment, the ACCC has considered
the information and documents that were submitted with the
notification form, information from third partes and publicly available
information.
The ACCC has determined that the Acquisition may be put into
effect as it considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to have the
effect of substantially lessening competition in any market. In
reaching its decision, and based on the material before it, the ACCC
makes the following findings.
• The Parties do not compete closely as the generative engine
optimisation tools they supply have different use cases.
• The Parties would continue to face competition from multiple
alternative suppliers of generative engine optimisation service
suppliers in Australia.

A notifying party, or other person who has been allowed to do so by
the Australian Competition Tribunal, may apply for review if they are
dissatisfied with the determination. Pursuant to section 100C of the
Act, applications for review of the determination are to be made to
the Australian Competition Tribunal before the end of 14 calendar
days after this statement of reasons was included on the ACCC’s
Acquisitions Register. To confirm whether there has been any
application for review, please contact the Australian Competition
Tribunal.

(**Estia**) proposes to acquire substantially all of the business assets of three residential aged care homes from Norsan Pty Ltd (**Norsan**), being Chirnside Views Residential Aged Care, Park Lane Croydon Residential Aged Care and Willowbrae - Melton Residential Aged Care (the **Acquisition**).

Estia is a registered provider of residential care services under the *Aged Care Act 2024 (Cth)* (**ACA**). Estia and its wholly owned subsidiaries currently operate over 90 residential aged care homes across Victoria, New South Wales, Queensland, and South Australia.

Norsan is a small family-owned supplier of three residential aged care services operating in certain parts of metropolitan Melbourne and outer Melbourne suburbs. Norsan is also a registered provider of residential care services under the ACA.

Both Estia and Norsan are providers of residential aged care services in certain areas within, and in the outer suburbs of metropolitan Melbourne.

merger_description:
Henkel AG & Co. KGaA (**Henkel**) proposes to acquire all the shares in and sole control of ATP Adhesives Systems Group GmbH (together with subsidiaries, **ATP**) from ATP Adhesives Holdings LP, which pre-transaction is controlled by affiliates of Arsenal Capital Partners.

Henkel is a German public company listed on the Frankfurt Stock Exchange. Henkel’s Adhesive Technologies business unit is a producer of adhesives, sealants and functional coatings for industrial customers, as well as for consumers and craftsmen. Henkel’s Adhesive Technologies brands available in Australia include Loctite, Teroson, Bonderite, Clingtape, Technomelt, Aquence and Pritt. In Australia, Henkel supplies tapes and labels (**T&L**) adhesives and non-water-based pressure-sensitive adhesive (**PSA**) tapes.

ATP is a private company headquartered in Wollerau, Switzerland. ATP manufactures PSA tapes for various end uses. In Australia, ATP supplies only water-based PSA tapes.

events.title: Henkel - ATP Adhesives Systems - Questionnaire
events.url_gh: /mergers/MN-40005/Henkel - ATP Adhesives Systems - Questionnaire.pdf

events.title: Henkel - ATP Adhesives Systems - Phase 1 determination
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by a division of the Commission constituted by a direction issued pursuant to section 19 of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
Henkel AG & Co. KGaA (Henkel) proposes to acquire all the shares in
and sole control of ATP Adhesives Systems Group GmbH (together
with subsidiaries, ATP) from ATP Adhesives Holdings LP, which pre-
transaction is controlled by affiliates of Arsenal Capital Partners.

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABZE(1) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition may be put into effect.

Henkel is a German public company listed on the Frankfurt Stock
Exchange. Henkel’s Adhesive Technologies business unit is a
producer of adhesives, sealants and functional coatings for
industrial customers, as well as for consumers and craftspeople. In
Australia, Henkel supplies tapes and labels (T&L) adhesives and
non-water-based pressure-sensitive adhesive (PSA) tapes. PSA
tapes are tapes that form an adhesive bond upon the application of
pressure. Henkel’s Adhesive Technologies brands available in
Australia include Loctite, Teroson, Bonderite, Clingtape, Technomelt,
Aquence and Pritt.
ATP is a private company headquartered in Wollerau, Switzerland.
ATP manufactures PSA tapes for various end uses. In Australia, ATP
supplies only water-based PSA tapes to industrial customers.
Both Henkel and ATP primarily supply specialty PSA tapes as
opposed to more general-use PSA tapes such as masking tape or
packing tape. Speciality PSA tapes generally have specific use-cases
in certain industries such as automotive, medical, electronics and
construction. An example of a use-case in the automotive industry is
to attach interior components like carpet to a vehicle.

determination_table_content.item:
Overlap and relationship
between the parties
determination_table_content.details:
The parties overlap in the supply of PSA tapes globally, including in
Australia. T&L adhesives are a key input into PSA tapes.
There are narrower segments within the supply of PSA tapes,
including non-water-based PSA tapes and water-based PSA tapes.

When making a determination in Phase 1, the ACCC undertakes a
competition assessment and considers whether it is appropriate for
an acquisition to be approved or subject to further assessment in
Phase 2 in accordance with section 51ABZJ of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act). In doing so, the ACCC must have
regard to the object of the Act and all relevant matters, including the
interests of consumers.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notified acquisitions, see the ACCC’s merger assessment guidelines
and interim merger process guidelines.
In conducting its competition assessment, the ACCC has considered
the information and documents that were submitted with the
notification form, publicly available information, and feedback from
market participants.
The ACCC has determined that the Acquisition may be put into
effect as it considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to have the
effect of substantially lessening competition in any market. In
reaching its decision, and based on the material before it, the ACCC
makes the following findings.
• The aggregation in the supply of PSA tapes resulting from the
Acquisition appears low, and the merged entity would likely
continue to be constrained by alternative suppliers of PSA tapes
in Australia post-acquisition.
• The merged entity would be unlikely to have the ability and
incentive to foreclose rivals’ access to inputs (in particular T&L
adhesives) given there are strong alternative suppliers of T&L
adhesives and PSA tapes in Australia.

A notifying party, or other person who has been allowed to do so by
the Australian Competition Tribunal, may apply for review if they are
dissatisfied with the determination. Pursuant to section 100C of the
Act, applications for review of the determination are to be made to
the Australian Competition Tribunal before the end of 14 calendar
days after this statement of reasons was included on the ACCC’s
Acquisitions Register. To confirm whether there has been any
application for review, please contact the Australian Competition
Tribunal.

anzsic_codes.name: Human Pharmaceutical and Medicinal Product Manufacturing

merger_description:
Gilead Sciences, Inc. (Gilead), through its wholly owned subsidiary Ravens Sub, Inc. (Ravens Sub) proposes to acquire all the outstanding shares of Arcellx, Inc. (Arcellx) via a public cash tender offer. Following consummation of the offer, Ravens Sub will be merged with Arcellx, and Arcellx will continue as the surviving corporation and a wholly owned subsidiary of Gilead.

Gilead is a biopharmaceutical company incorporated in the United States with headquarters in Foster City, California. Globally, Gilead’s activities focus on advancing medicines to prevent and treat life-threatening diseases, including HIV, liver disease, coronavirus disease 2019 (COVID-19), inflammation and cancer. Its primary subsidiary in Australia is Gilead Sciences Pty. Ltd., through which Gilead supplies Australian customers with a range of medicines, including, but not limited to cell therapies for oncology indications.

Arcellx is a clinical-stage biotechnology company incorporated in the United States, with headquarters in Redwood City, California. It is focused on developing immunotherapies for patients with cancer and other incurable diseases.

Arcellx’s most advanced development program is a D-Domain-based chimeric antigen receptor T‑cell (CAR-T) therapy that targets the B-cell maturation antigen (or BCMA) for treatment of multiple myeloma. This technology is now called anitocabtagene autoleucel or anito-cel.

Gilead’s wholly owned subsidiary, Kite, and Arcellx co-develop and co-commercialise anito-cell and potential future next-generation autologous and non-autologous CAR-T cell therapy products for the treatment of multiple myeloma. Outside the United States, Gilead has the exclusive right to apply for regulatory approval, manufacture and commercialise any anito-cel products developed as cancer treatments, including for indications resulting from other clinical trials. Five of the locations for a Phase 3 clinical trial are in Australia (Royal Prince Alfred Hospital and Royal North Shore Hospital in New South Wales, Royal Adelaide Hospital in South Australia, Epworth HealthCare in Victoria, and Sir Charles Gairdner Hospital in Western Australia).

Gilead’s blood cancer treatment products marketed in Australia are Yescarta (Anti-CD19 CAR-T), Tecartus (Anti-CD19 CAR-T), and Zydelig (P13K inhibitor). Gilead does not have any manufacturing or R&D facilities in Australia. It runs clinical trials in Australia and employs an operations team to support those trials.

Arcellx’s operations are limited to the United States, it has not generated revenues from the sale of products in Australia (or anywhere else) to date, and its only indirect nexus to Australia is limited to the clinical trials exclusively run by Gilead. Its clinical and pre-clinical programs are narrowly focused on specific types of cell therapies, mainly for the treatment of cancer, and primarily, blood cancers.

events.title: Gilead - Arcellx - Phase 1 determination
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Williams pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
Gilead Sciences, Inc. (Gilead), through its wholly owned subsidiary
Ravens Sub, Inc. (Ravens Sub) proposes to acquire all the
outstanding shares of Arcellx, Inc. (Arcellx) via a public cash tender
offer. Following consummation of the offer, Ravens Sub will be
merged with Arcellx, and Arcellx will continue as the surviving
corporation and a wholly owned subsidiary of Gilead.

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABZE(1) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition may be put into effect.

The acquirer, Gilead, is a biopharmaceutical company incorporated
in the United States with headquarters in Foster City, California.
Globally, Gilead’s activities focus on advancing medicines to prevent
and treat life-threatening diseases, including HIV, liver disease,
coronavirus disease 2019 (COVID-19), inflammation and cancer. Its
primary subsidiary in Australia is Gilead Sciences Pty. Ltd., through
which Gilead supplies Australian customers with a range of
medicines, including, but not limited to cell therapies for oncology
indications. In addition, Gilead runs clinical trials in Australia and
employs an operations team to support those trials.
Arcellx, the target, is a clinical-stage biotechnology company
incorporated in the United States, with headquarters in Redwood
City, California. It is focused on developing immunotherapies for
patients with cancer and other incurable diseases.
Arcellx’s operations are limited to the United States. It has not
generated revenues from the sale of products in Australia to date,
and its nexus to Australia is limited to clinical trials exclusively run
by Gilead. Its clinical and pre-clinical programs focus on products for
the treatment of cancer indications, primarily multiple myeloma,
which are co-developed and co-commercialised by Gilead and
Arcellx through an existing agreement. Outside the United States,
Gilead has the exclusive right to apply for regulatory approval,
manufacture and commercialise any anito-cel products developed
as cancer treatments, including for indications resulting from other
clinical trials.

determination_table_content.item:
Overlap and relationship
between the parties
determination_table_content.details:
There is no existing horizontal overlap between Gilead and Arcellx in
Australia.
As described above, there is an existing relationship between Gilead
and Arcellx allowing Gilead to exclusively commercialise jointly
developed products in Australia.

When making a determination in Phase 1, the Australian Competition
and Consumer Commission (ACCC) undertakes a competition
assessment and considers whether it is appropriate for an
acquisition to be approved or subject to further assessment in
Phase 2 in accordance with section 51ABZJ of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act). In doing so, the ACCC must have
regard to the object of the Act and all relevant matters, including the
interests of consumers.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notified acquisitions, see the ACCC’s merger assessment guidelines
and interim merger process guidelines.
In conducting its competition assessment, the ACCC has considered
the information and documents that were submitted with the
notification form.
The ACCC has determined that the Acquisition may be put into
effect as it considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to have the
effect of substantially lessening competition in any market. In
reaching its decision, and based on the material before it, the ACCC
makes the following findings.
• There are no competitive overlaps between Gilead and Arcellx in
the research, development or marketing of multiple myeloma
cell therapies in Australia.

A notifying party, or other person who has been allowed to do so by
the Australian Competition Tribunal, may apply for review if they are
dissatisfied with the determination. Pursuant to section 100C of the
Act, applications for review of the determination are to be made to
the Australian Competition Tribunal before the end of 14 calendar
days after this statement of reasons was included on the ACCC’s
Acquisitions Register. To confirm whether there has been any
application for review, please contact the Australian Competition
Tribunal.
