targets.name: STOCKLAND LAND LEASE MANAGEMENT PTY LIMITED
Stockland LLC No. 2 Pty Ltd (as trustee for the Stockland LLC St Germain Trust)

merger_description:
As part of its activities, Stockland Corporation Ltd and Stockland Trust (**Stockland**) operates a land lease communities (**LLC**) business, which currently comprises a portfolio of 39 LLC projects nationally, including both established communities and communities under development/in planning.

Stockland proposes, as a restructure, to transfer the land and development assets from two Stockland LLC projects, Halcyon Jardin and Halcyon Evergreen (in Clyde and Clyde North in Victoria), from Stockland controlled trusts/entities to newly established special purpose trusts (the **Acquisition**):

* The Jardin and Evergreen land will be transferred to SMLLP Asset Pty Ltd as trustee for the SMLLP Jardin Trust (**Jardin Land Buyer**) and SMLLP Asset Pty Ltd as trustee for the SMLLP Evergreen Trust (**Evergreen Land Buyer**) respectively.
* The Jardin and Evergreen development rights will be granted and development 'work in progress' will be transferred to SMLLP Sub Pty Ltd as trustee of the SMLLP Development Sub Trust (**Development Buyer**).

At the time of the Acquisition, the Jardin Land Buyer, Evergreen Land Buyer and Development Buyer will be (indirectly) controlled by Stockland. As newly established trusts, the Jardin and Evergreen Land Buyers and Development Buyer have no existing activities. Stockland will, post transfers, continue to develop the Jardin and Evergreen projects.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Woodward pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
Stockland Corporation Ltd and Stockland Trust (together Stockland)
applied for a notification waiver in respect of SMLLP Asset Pty Ltd’s
and SMLLP Sub Pty Ltd (Stockland Buyers) proposed acquisition of
the land and development rights of the Halcyon Jardin and Halcyon
Evergreen projects, currently held by Stockland LLC No.2 Pty Ltd and
Stockland Land Lease Management Pty Limited (Stockland Sellers),
as described in the transaction documents provided as part of the
application (the Acquisition).

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be
notified.
13 February 2026

The acquirer, Stockland, operates a land lease communities (LLC)
business, which currently comprises a portfolio of 39 LLC projects
nationally, including both established communities and communities
under development/in planning.
The Stockland Buyers are newly established trusts which will be
(indirectly) controlled by Stockland.
The Stockland Sellers are entities indirectly, wholly owned by
Stockland.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian
Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered
the information provided with the notification waiver application and
had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition
and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).
Based on the information provided in the application, the ACCC
considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material
lessening of competition. In particular:
a. The Acquisition involves the transfer of assets between
Stockland-controlled entities.
b. There is no horizontal consolidation or removal of a
competitor as a result of the Acquisition.
Given the above considerations that material competition concerns
are unlikely to arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is
not required to be notified.
In these circumstances, the ACCC does not consider it necessary to
reach a concluded view on the likelihood of the notification
thresholds being met.
The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the
object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting
competition.
The ACCC notes that it also considered the potential application of
section 51ABD (internal restructures and reorganisations) of the Act.
The application states that, on the facts of this matter, including the
particular entities and structures involved, it was not clear that the
Acquisition fell within that provision. Given this uncertainty, the
ACCC considered it appropriate to assess the notification waiver
application on the basis that the acquisition provisions in the Act
applied to the Acquisition.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notification waiver applications and to assessing competition
effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on
notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

merger_description:
Premier Fresh Australia Pty Ltd (**Premier Fresh**) proposes to purchase 100% of the shares in the following entities that are owned by Carmelo De Domenico and C.A. De Domenico Pty Ltd (the **Vendor**):

(a)        North Queensland IQF Pty Ltd, and

(b)        Double D Produce Pty Ltd

(collectively, the **Domenico Group**),

as well as the business, land and assets owned and operated by the Domenico Group.

The Domenico Group and the Vendor are involved in the growing, packing and value add processing for capsicums located in Ayr, Queensland.

Premier Fresh, through its related entities, has had a long-standing commercial relationship with the Domenico Group, with Premier Fresh historically purchasing and marketing a majority of the Domenico Group’s seasonal capsicum production under a marketing agreement.

Premier Fresh, through its related entities known as the Premier Fresh Group, is a horticulture marketer and supply chain operator that operates across a number of fruit and vegetable supply lines.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by a division of the Commission constituted by a direction issued pursuant to section 19 of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
Premier Fresh Australia Pty Ltd (Premier Fresh) applied for a
notification waiver in respect of its proposed acquisition of 100% of
the shares in North Queensland IQF Pty Ltd and Double D Produce
Pty Ltd (collectively, the Domenico Group), as well as the business,
land and assets owned and operated by the Domenico Group, as
described in the transaction documents provided as part of the
application (the Acquisition).

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABV(1)(b) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) not to make the notification waiver
determination applied for.
12 March 2026

The acquirer, Premier Fresh, is a horticulture marketer and supply
chain operator that operates across a number of fruit and vegetable
supply lines, including capsicums. Premier Fresh, through its related
entities, has had a long-standing commercial relationship with the
Domenico Group, with Premier Fresh historically purchasing and
marketing a majority of the Domenico Group’s seasonal capsicum
production under a marketing agreement.
The target, the Domenico Group, and the vendor, Carmelo De
Domenico and C.A. De Domenico Pty Ltd, are involved in the growing,
packing and value add processing for capsicums located in
Queensland.
Premier Fresh and the Domenico Group overlap in the supply of
capsicums in Australia.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian
Competition and Consumer Commission (ACCC) has considered the
information provided with the notification waiver application and had
regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Act.
Based on the information provided in the application, the ACCC
considers that, if the Acquisition were put into effect, the notification
thresholds would be met.
The ACCC is unable to reach a view that the Acquisition would not be
likely to raise competition issues in any market based on the
information provided in the application. It considers that the
Acquisition warrants consideration through the notification process,
including further information gathering and consultation with third
parties. This may include a focus on:
a. the extent of horizontal competitive overlap and the nature
of the vertical relationship between Premier Fresh and the
Domenico Group in the production and supply of capsicums
in Australia is unclear based on the application
b. the potential for foreclosure, noting that Premier Fresh is one
of the largest horticultural wholesalers.
c. the geographic dimensions of the markets
d. the extent to which the parties are likely to be constrained by
alternative producers and suppliers of capsicums in
Australia.
Given the above, the ACCC has determined not to make the
notification waiver determination applied for.
The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the
object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting
competition.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notification waiver applications and to assessing competition
effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on
notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

merger_description:
Duncan Holdco III LLC, a holding company that is ultimately controlled by SoftBank Group Corp. (Japan) (**SoftBank**), proposes to acquire 100% of the issued and outstanding shares of common stock (on a fully diluted basis) of, and voting rights in, DigitalBridge Group Inc. (**DigitalBridge**) (the **Acquisition**).

The Acquisition includes an indirect acquisition of all of DigitalBridge’s interests in each of the relevant general partner entities which manage DigitalBridge investment funds, through Duncan Holdco III LLC.

DigitalBridge is a dedicated, global-scale digital infrastructure investment firm that invests, directly and through its managed funds and their portfolio companies, in five key areas: (i) data centres; (ii) cell towers; (iii) fibre networks; (iv) small cells; and (v) edge infrastructure. DigitalBridge is headquartered in Boca Raton, Florida, United States of America with key offices in New York, Los Angeles, London, Luxembourg and Singapore. DigitalBridge is publicly listed on the New York Stock Exchange.

SoftBank is a publicly traded global investor headquartered in Tokyo, Japan, with a varied portfolio of investments and subsidiaries focused on the technology sector, many of which are minority, non-controlled investments. Companies and investments controlled by SoftBank conduct business activities globally across a wide range of sectors, including online learning, mobile and fixed-line communications services, microelectronics, transportation, software-as-a-service, internet advertising and e-commerce, banking, payment and investment, IT-platforms, artificial intelligence and mobile robots.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Williams pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
Duncan Holdco III LLC (Duncan Holdco 3) applied for a notification
waiver in respect of its proposed acquisition of 100% of the issued
and outstanding shares of common stock (on a fully diluted basis)
of, and voting rights in, DigitalBridge Group Inc. (DigitalBridge)
through 2 coordinated reverse subsidiary mergers, as described in
the transaction documents provided as part of the application (the
Acquisition). Duncan Holdco 3 is ultimately a subsidiary of SoftBank
Group Corp. (Japan) (SoftBank).
The Acquisition involves Duncan Sub I Inc., a wholly owned
subsidiary of Duncan Holdco 3, merging with and into DigitalBridge,
with DigitalBridge surviving as a wholly owned direct subsidiary of
Duncan Holdco 3. Immediately following, Duncan Sub II LLC, a wholly
owned subsidiary of Duncan Sub I Inc., will merge with and into
DigitalBridge Operating Company, LLC, with DigitalBridge Operating
Company, LLC surviving as a direct subsidiary of DigitalBridge.
As a result of the Acquisition, each of DigitalBridge and DigitalBridge
Operating Company, LLC will become solely controlled, indirect
subsidiaries of SoftBank.

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be
notified.
24 March 2026

SoftBank is a publicly traded global investor headquartered in Tokyo,
Japan, with a varied portfolio of investments and subsidiaries
focused on the technology sector. Companies and investments
controlled by SoftBank conduct business activities globally across a
wide range of sectors, including online learning, mobile and fixed-line
communications services, microelectronics, transportation,
software-as-a-service, internet advertising and e-commerce, banking,
payment and investment, IT platforms, artificial intelligence and
mobile robots.
The acquirer, Duncan Holdco 3, is a holding company created for the
purposes of the Acquisition and is ultimately a subsidiary controlled
by SoftBank.
The target, DigitalBridge, is a dedicated, global-scale digital
infrastructure investment firm that invests, directly and through its
managed funds and their portfolio companies, in data centres, cell
towers, fibre networks, small cells, and edge infrastructure.
DigitalBridge is headquartered in Boca Raton, Florida, United States
of America with key offices in New York, Los Angeles, London,
Luxembourg and Singapore. DigitalBridge is publicly listed on the
New York Stock Exchange.
Both SoftBank and DigitalBridge have connected entities that supply
internet connectivity services in Australia.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian
Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered
the information provided with the notification waiver application, and
had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition
and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).
Based on the information currently before it, the ACCC considers that
the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material lessening of
competition. In particular:
a. there is a small horizontal overlap between the connected
entities of SoftBank and DigitalBridge in the supply of
internet connectivity services in Australia
b. SoftBank and DigitalBridge have a small presence in the
supply of internet connectivity services in Australia
c. the presence of alternative suppliers of internet connectivity
services in Australia.
The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the
Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds
determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.
While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely
to be met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to
arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required
to be notified.
The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the
object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting
competition.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notification waiver applications and to assessing competition
effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on
notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

merger_description:
Enverus, Inc. (**Enverus**), a Texas corporation, proposes to acquire 100% of the issued share capital of SBS Optionco, LLC, a Delaware limited liability company, and SBS Blocker, LLC, a Delaware limited liability company (collectively, with each of their direct and indirect subsidiaries, **Spatial Business Systems**).

Enverus is a global Software-as-a-Service and data provider to the energy industry. Founded in 1999, and headquartered in Austin, Texas, Enverus offers a platform that integrates analytics, supply chain management, and commercial intelligence tools for oil and gas, power, utilities and renewable energy customers. Enverus is controlled by funds advised and/or managed by affiliates of Blackstone, Inc.

Spatial Business Systems is a global business headquartered in Littleton, Colorado in the United States. Spatial Business Systems supplies design software for utilities and critical network infrastructure assets to customers across electric and gas utilities, engineering and construction firms and broadband infrastructure operators. Spatial Business Systems’ software solutions are designed to automate and streamline engineering and design workflows, integrate spatial and design data and improve design effectiveness.

In Australia, Spatial Business Systems provides distribution design and substation services to customers.

7 April 2026

The acquirer, Enverus, is a global Software-as-a-Service and data
provider to the energy industry. Enverus was founded in 1999 and is
headquartered in Austin, Texas. Enverus offers a platform that
integrates analytics, supply chain management, and commercial
intelligence tools for oil and gas, power, utilities and renewable
energy customers.
Enverus is controlled by funds advised and/or managed by affiliates

solutions are designed to automate and streamline engineering and

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian

Based on the information provided in the application, the ACCC

to be notified.
The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the
object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting
competition.

merger_description:
Wentworth Capital Investment Management Pty Ltd (together with its holding companies and subsidiaries, **Wentworth**) is an Australian asset manager based in Sydney which specialises in real estate. Its core activities are asset management, real estate private equity and real estate private credit.

Funds managed by Wentworth will acquire 100% of the hotel businesses and related assets of Novotel Darling Harbour and Ibis Darling Harbour (**Hotel Businesses**) from Pearl Hotels D 1993 Pty Limited and the leasehold interests in the land on which the Hotel Businesses operate (**Property**) from The Trust Company (Australia) Limited in its capacity as trustee of the Pearl Hotels ZC 2013 Trust.

The Hotel Businesses currently offer hotel accommodation services (and ancillary services related to the operation of a hotel) under brands associated with the Accor Group, which will continue to manage the hotels post-acquisition.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Williams pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
Wentworth Inn Prop TC Pty Ltd, Wentworth Inn OpCo I Pty Ltd and
Wentworth Inn OpCo II Pty Ltd (the Acquirers) applied for a
notification waiver in respect of their proposed acquisition of:
• 100% of the hotel businesses and related assets of Novotel
Darling Harbour and Ibis Darling Harbour (Hotel Businesses)
from Pearl Hotels D 1993 Pty Limited
• the leasehold interests in the land on which the Hotel Businesses
operate (the Property) from The Trust Company (Australia)
Limited in its capacity as trustee of the Pearl Hotels ZC 2013
Trust
as described in the transaction documents provided as part of the
application (the Acquisition).

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be
notified.
7 April 2026

The Acquirers are funds managed by Wentworth Capital Investment
Management Pty Ltd (together with its holding companies and
subsidiaries, Wentworth). Wentworth is an Australian asset manager
based in Sydney which specialises in real estate. Its core activities
are asset management, real estate private equity and real estate
private credit.
The Hotel Businesses offer hotel accommodation services and
ancillary services related to the operation of a hotel under brands
associated with the Accor Group (which will continue to manage the
hotels post-Acquisition).
Wentworth states that it does not hold any interests in hotel or other
short-term accommodation businesses in Australia. However, one
non-controlling co-investor in the Acquirers, Mulpha Australia
Limited (which is ultimately owned by Mulpha International Bhd
(together, Mulpha)), has interests in the InterContinental Sydney at
Circular Quay and a proposed venue in Sussex Street, Sydney.
Mulpha is an international real estate and hospitality investor
headquartered in Malaysia.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian
Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered
the information provided with the notification waiver application and
had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition
and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).
Based on the information currently before it, the ACCC considers that
the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material lessening of
competition. In particular:
• there appears to be no, or a very limited, horizontal overlap
between Wentworth and the Hotel Businesses
• there are alternative suppliers of short-term accommodation and
ancillary services in Sydney, including in Darling Harbour
• the Accor Group will continue to operate and manage the Hotel
Businesses post-Acquisition.
In these circumstances, the ACCC does not consider it necessary to
reach a concluded view on the likelihood of the notification
thresholds being met.
Given that material competition concerns are unlikely to arise, the
ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required to be
notified.
The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the
object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting
competition.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notification waiver applications and to assessing competition
effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on
notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

merger_description:
Coforge Limited (**Coforge**) proposes to acquire Encora US Holdco, Inc. and Encora Holdings Limited (together, **Encora**). As consideration, Encora Holdco Limited and AI Altius Parent (**Cayman**) Limited (together, the Sellers) propose to acquire a non-controlling, minority interest in Coforge.

Coforge is a global technology services and solutions provider based in India. Coforge offers a broad range of technology consultancy services and solutions to business customers. Coforge operates across 25 countries, including Australia.

Encora is a global digital engineering services company specialising in supplying a narrow subset of technology consultancy services and solutions to business customers (namely, software product development). Encora is based in the United States and operates across a number of countries globally, with a limited presence in Australia.

The Sellers are indirectly owned by private equity funds managed and/or advised by Advent International, L.P. and/or its affiliates (**Advent**).

Advent is a private equity investment firm based in the United States. Advent is focused on equity stakes and the management of investment funds in various sectors, including healthcare, industrial, technology, retail, consumer, and business and financial services.

13 February 2026

Coforge is a global technology services and solutions provider based
in India. Coforge offers a broad range of technology consultancy

object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting

merger_name: Australian Retirement Trust – Interests in non-rental income (Westfield Sydney)

other_parties.name: THE TRUSTEE FOR AUSTRALIAN RETIREMENT TRUST

merger_description:
Australian Retirement Trust Pty Ltd as trustee of the Australian Retirement Trust (**ART**) is an Australian superannuation fund. ART manages retirement savings for >2.4 million members and >207,000 participating employers across Australia as at 30 June 2025. ART has limited interests in a small number of shopping centres in Sydney and New South Wales.

Scentre Group Limited (ASX: SCG) (**Scentre Group**) is an ASX-listed Australian real estate investment trust established in 2014. Scentre Group owns, develops and manages a portfolio of 42 Westfield shopping centres across Australia and New Zealand, including Westfield Sydney. Its business encompasses property investment, development, design, construction, leasing and asset management.

ART (through a wholly owned trust) has entered into an agreement with various wholly owned Scentre Group entities to acquire either:

* a 19.9% interest in certain digital advertising assets located at Westfield Sydney, and the non-rental revenue associated with certain digital and other advertising within Westfield Sydney, valet services, gift cards and other ancillary or incidental non-rental income; or
* only the non-rental revenue associated with certain digital and other advertising within Westfield Sydney, valet services, gift cards and other ancillary or incidental non-rental income
  
  (the **Acquisition**).

The Acquisition is related to ART’s proposed acquisition of a 19.9% interest in the property trusts that own the various parcels of land that comprise Westfield Sydney, announced 23 December 2025.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Woodward pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
QIC TT Company Pty Ltd (QIC), an indirectly wholly owned entity of
the Australian Retirement Trust Pty Ltd as trustee of the Australian
Retirement Trust (ART), applied for a notification waiver. The
application was made in respect of ART’s proposed acquisition
(through a wholly owned entity) from Scentre Group Limited (Scentre
Group) of:
• a 19.9% interest in certain digital advertising assets located
at Westfield Sydney, and the non-rental revenue associated
with certain digital and other advertising within Westfield
Sydney, valet services, gift cards and other ancillary or
incidental non-rental income; or
• only the non-rental revenue associated with certain digital
and other advertising within Westfield Sydney, valet
services, gift cards and other ancillary or incidental non-
rental income,
as described in the transaction documents provided as part of the
application (the Acquisition).

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be
notified.
20 February 2026

ART is one of Australia’s largest superannuation funds. ART
manages retirement savings for more than 2.4 million members and
over 207,000 participating employers across Australia as at 30 June
2025.
The vendor, Scentre Group, is an Australian real estate investment
trust, and a top 30 ASX-listed stapled property group. Scentre Group
owns, develops, and manages a portfolio of 42 Westfield shopping
centres (including Westfield Sydney) across Australia and New
Zealand. Scentre Group’s business encompasses property
investment, development, design, construction, leasing, and asset
management.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian
Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered
the information provided with the notification waiver application and
had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition
and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).
Based on the information provided by the applicant, the ACCC
considers that the Acquisition (with or without the digital advertising
assets) is unlikely to give rise to any material lessening of
competition. In particular:
a. the Acquisition gives rise to a limited horizontal overlap in
the supply of out-of-home advertising, gift cards, and other
ancillary or incidental services provided at shopping centres
b. any increment to ART’s share of revenue in the above
services arising from the Acquisition is low
c. the presence of alternative suppliers of advertising
opportunities, gift cards, and shopping centre ancillary
services.
In these circumstances, the ACCC does not consider it necessary to
reach a concluded view on the likelihood of the notification
thresholds being met.
Given that material competition concerns are unlikely to arise, the
ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required to be
notified.
The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the
object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting
competition.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notification waiver applications and to assessing competition
effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on

merger_description:
Lecta Limited and Lecta Paper Industries Intermediate Financing S.à r.l. have agreed with certain creditors of the Lecta Group to restructure the group’s financial indebtedness. As part of that restructure, funds managed by subsidiaries of Apollo Global Management (together with its indirect subsidiaries managing the investment funds, **Apollo**) will indirectly acquire sole control over Lecta Paper Industries Intermediate Financing S.à r.l. (together with its subsidiaries, **Lecta**) by way of a debt-to-equity swap (the **Acquisition**).

The Acquisition will be effected by a new holding company, Reed Bidco Limited (**BidCo**), acquiring all of the common equity in Lecta and issuing new equity to the funds managed by Apollo, among other creditors of Lecta.

Apollo is a high-growth global alternative asset manager headquartered in New York City, New York. Apollo offers investment management and financial planning services to individuals and institutional investors.

Lecta is a manufacturer and supplier of specialty papers for labels, flexible packaging and other high value-added print segments, as well as coated and uncoated paper for publishing. Lecta has its main operating offices in Barcelona, Spain and operates several production facilities in Europe, including manufacturing facilities producing pulp, base paper, specialty papers, self-adhesive papers and coated woodfree paper, with seven mills across Spain, France and Italy and a self-adhesive converting centre in Germany.

centre in Germany.

anzsic_codes.name: Engineering Design and Engineering Consulting Services

merger_description:
The Acquirer, Global Garella, S.L., a wholly-owned subsidiary of Colliers International Group Inc., (**CIGI**) proposes to acquire 100% of the shares in Ayesa Engineering, S.A.U. (**Ayesa**) from Ayesa Inversiones, S.L.U.

CIGI is a global, diversified professional services and investment management company that operates through three divisions (Real Estate Services, Engineering and Investment Management) in over 70 countries. In Australia, its engineering consultancy services include engineering design services and site supervision and project management services, in particular, civil-based engineering consultancy services for projects in the property and buildings and water sectors.

Ayesa provides engineering consultancy services, including engineering design and site supervision and project management services, in various countries worldwide. In Australia, Ayesa supplies only engineering design services, in particular, mechanical, electrical and plumbing design services for projects in the buildings and cities, industry, and transportation sectors.

In Australia, CIGI and Ayesa overlap in the supply of engineering consultancy services. CIGI supplies both engineering design services and site supervision and project management services, in particular, civil-based engineering consultancy services for projects in the buildings and cities and water segments. While Ayesa supplies site supervision and project management services in other countries, in Australia, its engineering consultancy services are limited to engineering design services, in particular, mechanical, electrical and plumbing design services in the buildings and cities, industry, and transportation sectors.

merger_name: MA Financial Group (Redcape Fund) - Hotel Brunswick

merger_description:
MAHPT TT Pty Ltd (as trustee for Redcape Hotel Property Trust) and Redcape Hotel Group Pty Ltd (the **Acquirers**) propose to acquire 100% of the freehold land interest, buildings, licences and business assets (both tangible and intangible) of Hotel Brunswick from Brunswick Hotel TT Pty Ltd as trustee for Brunswick Hotel Sub-Trust and Brunswick Hotel Opco Pty Ltd (the **Acquisition**).

Hotel Brunswick is a community, food, beverage and entertainment venue located in Brunswick Heads, New South Wales. It also operates a bottle shop.

The Acquirers are part of MA Financial Group Limited’s (ASX:MAF) Redcape Fund, which owns and operates community pubs and hotels. The Redcape Fund portfolio currently has 34 hotels in New South Wales, Queensland and Victoria.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Williams pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
MAHPT TT Pty Ltd (as trustee for Redcape Hotel Property Trust) and
Redcape Hotel Group Pty Ltd (the Acquirers) propose to acquire
100% of the freehold land interest, buildings, licenses and business
assets (both tangible and intangible) of Hotel Brunswick from
Brunswick Hotel TT Pty Ltd as trustee for Brunswick Hotel Sub-Trust
and Brunswick Hotel Opco Pty Ltd (the Sellers), as described in the
transaction documents provided as part of the application (the
Acquisition).

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be
notified.
20 March 2026

The Acquirers are part of MA Financial Group Limited’s (ASX:MAF)
(MA Financial) Redcape Fund, which owns and operates community
pubs and hotels. The Redcape Fund portfolio currently has 34 hotels
in New South Wales, Queensland and Victoria.
The target, Hotel Brunswick, is a community, food, beverage and
entertainment venue located in Brunswick Heads, New South Wales.
It also operates a Cellarbrations bottle shop. The Hotel Brunswick is
located at located at 2-4 Mullumbumbi Street, Brunswick Head, NSW
2483.
The Sellers are part of MA Financial’s Brunswick Fund, a single asset
investment fund.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian
Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered
the information provided with the notification waiver application and
had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition
and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).
Based on the information provided in the application, the ACCC
considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material
lessening of competition. In particular:
a. the Acquisition involves the transfer of assets between MA
Financial-controlled entities.
b. there is no horizontal consolidation or removal of a
competitor as a result of the Acquisition.
c. the presence of alternative suppliers of products and
services available at hotels and hospitality venues, and retail
suppliers of packaged liquor for offsite consumption.
The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the
Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds
determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.
While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely
to be met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to
arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required
to be notified.
The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the
object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting
competition.
The ACCC notes that it also considered the potential application of
section 51ABD (internal restructures and reorganisations) of the Act.
The application noted the potential application of this provision and
the ACCC considered that the Acquisition might fall within it.
However, based on facts of this matter and having regard to the
entities and structures involved, the ACCC considered it was
appropriate to make a determination on the basis that the
acquisition provisions in the Act applied to the Acquisition.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notification waiver applications and to assessing competition
effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on
notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

AZ Next Generation Advisory Pty Ltd (**AZ Next Generation Advisory**) proposes to acquire 100% of the shares in BMM Partners Pty Ltd, trading as Intend Financial (**Intend Financial**).

AZ Next Generation Advisory is an Australian professional services advisory company established in 2014 that owns approximately 48 financial planning and accounting businesses with operations and customers across Australia, including in Queensland, Victoria and Tasmania (where Intend Financial has a presence). AZ Next Generation Advisory has approximately 404 licensed financial planners across its network.

Intend Financial is a financial planning business established in 2021 that provides financial planning services from four offices, in Victoria (Mornington and Hawthorn East), Queensland (Gold Coast) and Tasmania (Launceston), to customers across Australia. Intend Financial has 10 licensed financial planners.

targets.name: TELEGRAPH ROAD MEDICAL SERVICES PTY LTD
The Trustee for TELEGRAPH ROAD MEDICAL SERVICES UNIT TRUST

merger_description:
Ochre Health Pty Limited (**Ochre Health**) proposes to acquire the business and all of the assets of the Telegraph Road Clinic (**TRC**) in Bracken Ridge, Queensland, through a business and assets acquisition agreement (the **Acquisition**).

The acquirer, Ochre Health, supports independent doctors to provide general practice medical services in regional, rural and urban communities around Australia. Ochre Health currently operates 68 medical centres and urgent care clinics located in the ACT, NSW, Queensland, Tasmania and Victoria, but currently does not operate any medical centres or urgent care clinics within 10km of TRC in Bracken Ridge, Queensland. Ochre Health Group’s medical recruitment business, Ochre Recruitment, also works with hospitals and medical practices across Australia and NZ to source and place locum and permanent doctors across a range of specialities.

Ochre Health is majority owned and controlled by Genesis Capital (**Genesis**), an Australian-based private equity firm that invests in businesses operating in the healthcare sector. Genesis’s investments also include Holdsworth House Medical Group (**Holdsworth House**), which operates a general medical practice in Fortitude Valley, Brisbane and another general medical practice in Sydney which is co-located with a medical research centre, specialist medical clinic and dentistry clinic.

The target, TRC, is a family medical practice providing comprehensive general practice medical care from its clinic located in the Bracken Ridge Plaza, Shop 26, 250 Telegraph Road, Bracken Ridge, Queensland, and home visits to nearby patients (within a 3km radius of the clinic). TRC is owned and operated by Telegraph Road Medical Services Pty Ltd as trustee for Telegraph Road Medical Services Unit Trust.

merger_description:
The Carlyle Group Inc. (**Carlyle**) is a global investment firm headquartered in Washington DC, USA, with assets under management across three business segments: (i) Global Private Equity (including corporate private equity and real estate funds); (ii) Global Credit (including liquid credit, illiquid credit and real assets credit); and (iii) Carlyle AlpInvest (including primary, secondary, and co-investments, commingled funds, and separately managed accounts).

Peloton Computer Enterprises Ltd. (**Peloton**) is a global supplier of software predominantly used by exploration and production (**E&P**) operators in the upstream oil and gas industry. E&P is an early phase in the oil and gas industry concerned with locating and extracting raw materials.

Carlyle, via its controlled investment vehicle (CG Panther Parentco, L.P., **Panther**), proposes to acquire non-voting and non-convertible preferred equity in Peloton, as well as warrants that (if exercised) would provide Carlyle with up to c. 14% of the fully-diluted equity in Peloton (pro forma for the transaction). Carlyle will ultimately (via Panther) hold only (i) preferred equity and (ii) warrants amounting up to c. 14% holding of common equity interests in Peloton on a lookthrough basis.

The Property will be used by H-E Mining and H-E Parts International Mining Solutions Cooling Division Pty Ltd (**H-E Cooling**).

These companies are providers of parts, engineered components, and service support for heavy-duty equipment used in mining and related industries, supporting both surface and underground operations as well as crushing and materials processing applications.

ATB Morton is a commercial and industrial property developer.

determination_table_content.details:
H-E Parts International Mining Solutions Pty Ltd (H-E Mining) applied
for a notification waiver in respect of its proposed agreement for
lease with ATB Morton Pty Ltd (ATB Morton) in respect of land
located at 88 Michelmore Street, Paget, Mackay, Queensland (the
Property) and improvements to be constructed by ATB Morton, as
described in the transaction documents provided as part of the
application (the Acquisition).

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be
notified.
12 February 2026

The acquirer, H-E Mining, supplies parts, engineered components,
and service support for heavy-duty equipment used in mining and
related industries, supporting both surface and underground
operations as well as crushing and materials processing
applications. H-E Parts International Mining Solutions Cooling
Division Pty Ltd (H-E Cooling) provides cooling and heat-transfer
solutions for mining and heavy industrial equipment. HEP Australia
Holdings Pty Ltd is the parent entity of H-E Mining and H-E Cooling.
ATB Morton is a commercial and industrial property developer, and
will be the landlord of the Property. ATB Morton will construct a
purpose-built industrial warehouse and workshop facility on the
Property. ATB Morton will then lease the Property and improvements
constructed by ATB Morton to H-E Mining.
The Property will be used by H-E Mining and H-E Cooling, and would
be an input to their operations.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian
Competition and Consumer Commission (ACCC) has considered the
information provided with the notification waiver application and had
regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (Act).
Based on the information provided in the application and publicly
available information, the ACCC considers that the Acquisition is
unlikely to give rise to any material lessening of competition. In
particular:
a. There is no horizontal competitive overlap.
b. There appear to be alternative sites available for H-E
Mining’s and H-E Cooling’s competitors.
c. The Acquisition does not appear likely to otherwise limit or
prevent competition from rivals.
In these circumstances, the ACCC does not consider it necessary to
reach a concluded view on the likelihood of the notification
thresholds being met.
Given that material competition concerns are unlikely to arise, the
ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required to be
notified.
The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the
object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting
competition.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notification waiver applications and to assessing competition
effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on
notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

anzsic_codes.name: Computer System Design and Related Services

merger_description:
Telstra Limited (**Telstra**) is a leading Australian telecommunications and technology company, offering a range of communications services. Telstra provides consumer and business connectivity across mobile and fixed broadband, and delivers enterprise solutions including network, cloud, security, data and analytics, software development, and managed/professional services.

Infosys Technologies Limited (**Infosys**) is a global technology consultancy and outsourcing provider. It provides end-to-end solutions spanning consulting, technology, engineering and outsourcing services, and also offers software products and platforms. The company has a large global footprint, with operations across 59 countries.

Under an agreement, Telstra will outsource certain business functions to Infosys and Infosys will transform Telstra’s enterprise IT stack in respect of underlying carriage services supplied to certain Telstra Enterprise and Mid-market customers. As part of the agreement, Telstra and Infosys will acquire rights in relation to certain intellectual property.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by a division of the Commission constituted by a direction issued pursuant to section 19 of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
Telstra Limited (Telstra) applied for a notification waiver in respect
of a proposed agreement with Infosys Technologies Limited
(Infosys), to the extent it involves an acquisition of assets and/or
legal or equitable rights that are not property (the Acquisition). The
agreement provides for Telstra to outsource certain business
functions to Infosys and for Infosys to transform its Enterprise IT
stack in respect of underlying carriage services provided to certain
Telstra Enterprise and Mid-market customers, as described in the
transaction documents provided as part of the application. The
Acquisition includes Telstra and Infosys acquiring rights in relation
to certain intellectual property.

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be
notified.
19 March 2026

Telstra is an Australian telecommunications and technology
company, offering a range of communications services. Telstra
provides consumer and business connectivity across mobile and
fixed broadband, and delivers enterprise solutions including network,
cloud, security, data and analytics, software development, and
managed/professional services.
Infosys is a global technology consultancy and outsourcing provider.
It provides end-to-end solutions spanning consulting, technology,
engineering and outsourcing services, and also offers software
products and platforms. The company has a large global footprint,
with operations across 59 countries.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian
Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered
the information provided with the notification waiver application and
had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition
and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).
Based on the information provided, the ACCC considers that the
Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material lessening of
competition. In particular:
a. the Acquisition is not likely to result in a material increase in
concentration in the supply of the services offered by Telstra
and Infosys in Australia
b. there is a low risk of foreclosure resulting from exclusionary
conduct post-acquisition.
The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the
Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds
determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply. While
the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely to be
met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to arise,
the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required to be
notified.
The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the
object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting
competition.
The ACCC notes that it also considered whether the Acquisition fell
within the scope of sections 51ABU and 51ABV of the Act. Based on
the information provided by the parties and the facts of this matter,
in particular the assets and/or legal or equitable rights involved,
including intellectual property rights, the ACCC considered it
appropriate to make a determination in relation to the notification
waiver application.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notification waiver applications and to assessing competition
effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on
notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

merger_name: Hall & McLean – Freehold interest in commercial land in Wellcamp, QLD

other_parties.name: Ellerslie Free Range Farms Pty Ltd

anzsic_codes.name: Other Agriculture and Fishing Support Services

merger_description:
Hall & McLean Pty Ltd (**Hall & McLean**) proposes to acquire a 100% freehold interest in commercial land comprising Lot 8 on SP296133, known as 2 Foundation Street, Wellcamp, QLD 4350 (the **Property**), from Vision Street Property Pty Ltd (the **Vendor**) (the **Acquisition**).

Hall & McLean is a 50:50 joint venture between Ellerslie Free Range Farms Pty Ltd (**Ellerslie**) and the McLean family (through Alpair Pty Ltd).

Alpair Pty Ltd, is an egg producer based on the Darling Downs in Queensland

Ellerslie is an agribusiness entity operating principally in the chicken egg production sector.

Ellerslie is 60% owned by PSP Investments, with the remaining 40% held by DHP Consolidated Pty Ltd on behalf of the Hall family.

Ellerslie and Alpair Pty Ltd each have a 50% interest in Sunny Queen, an egg distributer.

The Property is located ~13km outside of Toowoomba in Queensland and currently used by Ellerslie as an egg grading and cold storage site.

Ellerslie has been renting the Property from the Vendor under a 5-year lease for the purposes of egg grading and cold storage for its egg production business. Prior to Ellerslie entering into that lease, the Property was vacant for a period of approximately 5 years.

Following completion of the Acquisition, Ellerslie will continue to use the Property for egg grading and cold storage, albeit as owner rather than lessee.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Williams pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
Hall & McLean Pty Ltd (Hall & McLean) applied for a notification
waiver in respect of its proposed acquisition of a 100% freehold
interest in commercial land comprising Lot 8 on SP296133, known
as 2 Foundation Street, Wellcamp QLD 4350 (the Property), from
Vision Street Property Pty Ltd (the Vendor), as described in the
transaction documents provided as part of the application (the
Acquisition).

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be
notified.
23 March 2026

The acquirer, Hall & McLean, is a 50:50 joint venture between
Ellerslie Free Range Farms Pty Ltd (Ellerslie) and the McLean family
(through Alpair Pty Ltd).
Alpair Pty Ltd, is an egg producer based in the Darling Downs region
in Queensland.
Ellerslie is an agribusiness entity operating principally in the chicken
egg production sector. Ellerslie layer farms produce caged, cage
free, free range, pasture raised and organic shell eggs.
Ellerslie is 60% owned by PSP Investments, with the remaining 40%
held by DHP Consolidated Pty Ltd on behalf of the Hall family.
Ellerslie and Alpair Pty Ltd each have a 50% interest in Sunny Queen,
an egg distributor.
Ellerslie has been renting the Property from the Vendor under a 5-
year lease for the purposes of egg grading and cold storage for its
egg production business.
The Vendor does not operate any egg farming or production
business in Australia.
The Property would be an input into the operations of Ellerslie.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian
Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered
the information provided with the notification waiver application and
certain publicly available information, and had regard to the factors
in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010
(Cth) (the Act).
Based on the information currently before it, the ACCC considers that
the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material lessening of
competition. In particular:
a. the Acquisition is an acquisition of land by an egg producer
from a party who is not an egg producer
b. Ellerslie has been using the land as a lessee for 5 years and
intends to continue using the land for the same purposes,
but as an owner
c. there appear to be alternative sites available for Ellerslie’s
and the McLean family’s competitors
In these circumstances, the ACCC does not consider it necessary to
reach a concluded view on the likelihood of the notification
thresholds being met.
Given that material competition concerns are unlikely to arise, the
ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required to be
notified.
The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the
object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting
competition.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notification waiver applications and to assessing competition
effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on
notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

merger_name: PT Asian Bulk Logistics – Engage Marine

other_parties.name: AUSTRALIAN GLOBAL MARINE AND LOGISTICS PTY LTD
Tug and Barge Services Pty Ltd

merger_description:
PT Asian Bulk Logistics, through its Australian subsidiary, Global Engage Marine Pty Ltd, proposes to acquire 100% of the issued share capital in Engage Marine TopCo Pty Ltd (**Engage Marine**).

Engage Marine provides terminal and harbour towage including the berthing and sailing of Cape-size, Panamax, and Handy-size vessels. Engage Marine also provides ad-hoc towage emergency response solutions in salvage situations. The main industry Engage Marine operates in is marine services (towage and port services).

PT Asian Bulk Logistics is an integrated logistics and infrastructure group supplying bulk logistics services, port services and marine services. It primarily operates in Indonesia.

Through one of its subsidiaries, Transhipment Services Australia, PT Asian Bulk Logistics operates a port-specific towage fleet supporting transhipment and logistics operations.

PT Asian Bulk Logistics operates two additional subsidiary companies in Australia – One Rail Australia and SCF Containers.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by a division of the Commission constituted by a direction issued pursuant to section 19 of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
Global Engage Marine Pty Ltd, a subsidiary of PT Asian Bulk
Logistics, applied for a notification waiver in respect of its proposed
acquisition of 100% of the issued share capital in Engage Marine
TopCo Pty Ltd (Engage Marine), as described in the transaction
documents provided as part of the application (the Acquisition).

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABV(1)(b) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) not to make the notification waiver
determination applied for.
19 March 2026

PT Asian Bulk Logistics is an integrated logistics and infrastructure
group supplying bulk logistics services, port services and marine
services. It primarily operates in Indonesia.
Through one of its subsidiaries, Transhipment Services Australia, PT
Asian Bulk Logistics operates a port-specific towage fleet supporting
transhipment and logistics operations. Transhipment Services
Australia primarily services Australian mining companies in northern
Queensland.
PT Asian Bulk Logistics also operates two additional subsidiary
companies in Australia, One Rail Australia and SCF Containers. One
Rail Australia is an Australian rail-based freight and logistics
company specializing in coal, bulk, and intermodal transport,
primarily operating in New South Wales and Queensland.
The target, Engage Marine, provides terminal and harbour towage
including the berthing and sailing of Cape-size, Panamax, and Handy-
size vessels. Engage Marine also provides ad-hoc towage
emergency response solutions in salvage situations. The main
industry Engage Marine operates in is marine services (towage and
port services). Engage Marine services mining companies, port
authorities, terminal operators, and vessels directly, at major ports
around Australia.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian
Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered
the information provided with the notification waiver application and
had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition
and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).
Based on the information provided in the application, including the
parties’ revenue in Australia and the transaction value, the ACCC
considers that, if the Acquisition were put into effect, the notification
thresholds would be met.
The ACCC is unable to reach a view that the Acquisition would not be
likely to raise competition issues in any market based on the
information provided. It considers that the Acquisition warrants
consideration through the notification process, including via further
information gathering and consultation with third parties. This may
include a focus on:
a. the extent of any potential horizontal overlap and/or
complementary relationship between PT Asian Bulk
Logistics (including its subsidiaries) and Engage Marine in
the supply of marine services, transhipment services, port
logistics, and freight and logistics, in Australia
b. the relevant geographic dimension of the supply of PT Asian
Bulk Logistics’ and Engage Marine’s services in Australia
c. the nature and extent of PT Asian Bulk Logistics’ and Engage
Marine’s supply of services to mining companies in Australia
d. the extent to which the parties are likely to face competition
from alternative suppliers of marine services, transhipment
services, and port logistics in Australia.
Given the above, the ACCC has determined not to make the
notification waiver determination applied for.
The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the
object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting
competition.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notification waiver applications and to assessing competition
effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on
notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

merger_name: Regent Motors Group – Esperance Motor Group (Esperance Toyota & Ford)

targets.name: BUVERIE PTY LTD as trustee for SCHWARZBACK FAMILY TRUST

merger_description:
Albany Motors Pty Ltd (**Albany Motors**), a member of Regent Motors Pty Ltd (**Regent Motors Group**), proposes to acquire 100% of the issued shares in Esperance Motor Group Pty Ltd (**Esperance Motor Group**) from Buverie Pty Ltd as trustee for the Schwarzback Family Trust.

Regent Motors Group owns and operates franchised motor vehicle dealerships in Western Australia and Northern Territory, and supplies new (by brand) and used motor vehicles, plus aftersales services including servicing and repairs, parts and accessories, fleet sales/service, and finance and insurance facilitation.

Esperance Motor Group operates a Toyota and Ford dealership in Esperance, Western Australia, supplying new and demonstrator Toyota and Ford vehicles, used vehicles, and aftersales services including servicing and repairs, parts and accessories, fleet activities, and finance and insurance facilitation.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Luke Woodward pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
Albany Motors Pty Ltd (Albany Motors) applied for a notification
waiver in respect of its proposed acquisition of 100% of the share
capital of Esperance Motor Group Pty Ltd (Esperance Motor Group),
as described in the transaction documents provided as part of the
application (the Acquisition).

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be
notified.
5 February 2026

The acquirer, Albany Motors, is a member of Regent Motors Pty Ltd
(Regent Motors Group). Regent Motors Group owns and operates
franchised motor vehicle dealerships across Western Australia and
Northern Territory, and supplies new and used motor vehicles
(including Toyota, Ford, Hyundai, Volkswagen, BMW, MG, Mini, Isuzu,
Renault, GMSV, Suzuki and Subaru vehicles), plus after sale services
including servicing and repairs, parts and accessories, fleet
sales/service, and finance and insurance facilitation.
The target, Esperance Motor Group, operates a Toyota and Ford
dealership in Esperance, Western Australia, supplying new and
demonstrator Toyota and Ford vehicles, used vehicles, and after sale
services including servicing and repairs, parts and accessories, fleet
activities, and finance and insurance facilitation.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian
Competition and Consumer Commission (ACCC) has considered the
information provided by the applicant, including in the notification
waiver application, and had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)
of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (Act).
Based on the information provided by the applicant, the ACCC
considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material
lessening of competition. In particular:
a. There is no horizontal overlap between Regent Motors Group
and Esperance Motor Group in the supply of new and used
motor vehicles and after sale services in Esperance and the
surrounding areas.
b. There is a potential horizontal overlap between Regent
Motors Group and Esperance Motor Group in the broader
area of southern WA. If Perth is excluded from this area,
there are independent alternatives in Albany, Kalgoorlie,
Katanning and Narrogin. If Perth is included in this area, the
merged parties would likely face competition from
numerous additional alternative dealerships in Perth.
c. Any increment to Regent Motors Group share of the supply
of Toyota and Ford vehicles in Western Australia is small.
The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the
Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds
determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.
While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely
to be met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to
arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required
to be notified.
The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the
object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting
competition.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notification waiver applications and to assessing competition
effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on
notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

anzsic_codes.name: Computer System Design and Related Services

merger_description:
Delinea Inc. (**Delinea**), a company ultimately controlled by funds managed and/or advised by affiliates of TPG Inc. (**TPG**), proposes to acquire 100% of the issued share capital in StrongDM, Inc (**StrongDM**).

TPG is a global alternative asset manager firm founded in San Francisco.

Delinea is a software business specialising in solutions for identity security through its identity platform that enables enterprises to securely vault credentials, efficiently manage access for human and machine identities, assign appropriate access levels, detect irregularities, manage identity lifecycles, and respond to threats in real-time.

StrongDM is a provider of just-in-time based Privileged Access Management (**PAM**) software primarily deployed and operated in cloud environments. StrongDM's products allow temporary access to IT resources which is granted at a specific time to a specific identify for a specific purpose.

Delinea and StrongDM supply software focused on validating the identity of users to enforce policies regarding the authorisation of users to specific IT resources and distributed business systems, known as Identity and Access Management (**IAM**) software. IAM products verify and manage digital identities and govern, administer, and enforce user and machine access rights to systems, applications, and data across on-premises and cloud environments. Delinea and StrongDM supply PAM solutions, which are a subset of IAM, which provide an elevated level of technical access.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by a division of the Commission constituted by a direction issued pursuant to section 19 of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
Delinea Inc. (Delinea) applied for a notification waiver in respect of
its proposed acquisition of 100% of the issued share capital in
StrongDM, Inc. (StrongDM), through a merger with, Spartan Merger
Sub, Inc., a wholly owned subsidiary of Delinea, as described in the
transaction documents provided as part of the application (the
Acquisition).

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be
notified.
3 February 2026

The acquirer, Delinea, is a software business specialising in
solutions for identity security through its identity platform that
enables enterprises to securely vault credentials, efficiently manage
access for human and machine identities, assign appropriate access
levels, detect irregularities, manage identity lifecycles, and respond
to threats in real-time. Delinea is a company ultimately controlled by
funds managed and/or advised by affiliates of TPG Inc. (TPG). TPG
is a global alternative asset manager firm founded in San Francisco.
The target, StrongDM, delivers just-in-time based privileged access
management (PAM) software primarily deployed and operated in
cloud environments. StrongDM's products allow temporary access
to IT resources which is granted at a specific time to a specific
identify for a specific purpose.
Delinea and StrongDM overlap in the supply of software focused on
validating the identity of users to enforce policies regarding the
authorisation of users to specific IT resources and distributed
business systems, known as Identity and Access Management (IAM)
software. IAM products verify and manage digital identities and
govern, administer, and enforce user and machine access rights to
systems, applications, and data across on-premises and cloud
environments. Delinea and StrongDM supply PAM solutions, which
are a subset of IAM, which provide an elevated level of technical
access.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian
Competition and Consumer Commission (ACCC) has considered the
information provided with the notification waiver application, public
information and other industry information held by the ACCC, and
had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition
and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (Act).
Based on the information outlined above, the ACCC considers that
the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material lessening of
competition. In particular:
a. StrongDM has a very small presence in Australia.
b. There are numerous alternative suppliers of IAM or PAM
software in Australia and globally.
c. While there is horizontal overlap between the software
supplied by Delinea and StrongDM, the increase in share of
supply in Australia arising from the Acquisition would be
very small.
d. Apart from Delinea, no horizontal overlap has been identified
between any of TPG’s other connected entities and
StrongDM.
The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the
Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds
determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.
While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely
to be met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to
arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required
to be notified.
The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the
object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting
competition.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notification waiver applications and to assessing competition
effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on
notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

merger_name: Australian Venue Co. – leasehold and certain other assets of Quay Restaurant, Sydney
merger_description:
AVC Operations Pty Limited (**AVC**) proposes to acquire a leasehold (via lease assignment), the benefit of a liquor licence and certain plant and equipment (with certain exclusions) of Quay Restaurant (**Quay**) from Q.R.A. Pty Limited (the **Acquisition**). The Vendor will not transfer the existing business of Quay to AVC, only the specific assets mentioned above.   

AVC is a subsidiary of Australian Venue Co. Holdings Limited which operates licensed venues throughout Australia and New Zealand, primarily pubs, as well as bars, clubs and licensed restaurants. AVC's venues are individually branded and mostly located in inner-city and metro areas, offering food, alcoholic and non-alcoholic beverages, function rooms, live entertainment and gaming facilities.   

Quay is situated on the Sydney harbourfront at Tenancy 6, Upper Level, Overseas Passenger Terminal, The Rocks. Quay Restaurant offers fine dining with views of the Sydney Opera House and Harbour Bridge.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Philip Williams pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
Australian Venue Co. Holdings Limited (Australian Venue Co), via
AVC Operations Pty Limited (AVC), applied for a notification waiver
in respect of its proposed acquisition of a leasehold, benefit of a
liquor licence and certain assets of Quay Restaurant (Quay), as
described in the transaction documents provided as part of the
application (the Acquisition).

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be
notified.
9 February 2026

The acquirer, Australian Venue Co, operates licensed venues
throughout Australia and New Zealand, primarily pubs, as well as
bars, clubs and licensed restaurants. Their venues are individually
branded and mostly located in inner-city and metro areas, offering
food, alcoholic and non-alcoholic beverages, function rooms, live
entertainment and gaming facilities.
The target, Quay, is situated on the Sydney harbourfront at Tenancy
6, Upper Level, Overseas Passenger Terminal, The Rocks. Quay
Restaurant is a contemporary restaurant that offers fine dining with
views of the Sydney Opera House and Harbour Bridge and plans to
cease trading in mid-February 2026.
Australian Venue Co, through its venue The Squire’s Landing, and
Quay both supply retail hospitality restaurants in the same building in
the Overseas Passenger Terminal.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian
Competition and Consumer Commission (ACCC) has considered the
information provided with the notification waiver application and had
regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (Act).
Based on the information provided in the application, the ACCC
considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material
lessening of competition. In particular:
• While there is horizontal overlap between Quay and
Australian Venue Co, the combined share of the parties in
the supply of retail hospitality restaurants in the Circular
Quay area of Sydney is low.
• The presence of alternative suppliers of retail hospitality
restaurants in proximity to the Circular Quay area of Sydney.
In these circumstances, the ACCC does not consider it necessary to
reach a concluded view on the likelihood of the notification
thresholds being met. Given that material competition concerns are
unlikely to arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not
required to be notified.
The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the
object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting
competition.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notification waiver applications and to assessing competition
effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on
notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.
