other_parties.name: Five V Frontier Fund UGP1 Pty Ltd

anzsic_codes.name: Other Auxiliary Finance and Investment Services
Computer System Design and Related Services

merger_description:
Five V Capital Pty Ltd (**Five V Capital**) is an Australian-incorporated private equity and venture capital firm with headquarters in Australia and New Zealand. Five V Capital manages a number of Five V funds, including Horizons Fund and Frontier Fund. Horizons Fund and Frontier Fund are investment funds that pursue a strategy of investing in private mid-market businesses.

Five V Capital and an incorporated company that is wholly owned by Horizons Fund and Frontier Fund, Overwatch BidCo Pty Ltd, propose to acquire the majority of the issued shares in FTP Group Holdings Pty Ltd and its subsidiaries (the **FTP Group**) from the existing shareholders of FTP Group Holdings Pty Ltd (the **Acquisition**).

FTP Group is based in Australia, Canada and New Zealand and operates under the business names ‘FTP Solutions’, ‘Faarm’ and ‘Yarn Mesh’.

FTP Solutions is a provider of integrated network and operational monitoring software to the global mining industry. Its flagship product, ‘IMS’, is a hardware agnostic software tool designed to monitor and visualise the network and operations of mine sites in real-time. IMS allows its users to quickly identify and resolve network issues that could result in operational downtime.

Faarm and Yarn Mesh operate in the agricultural sector. Faarm is a software platform that aggregates disparate farm data (e.g. water usage, fertiliser, soil quality, weather and livestock metrics) into a single interface. Yarn Mesh designs and manufactures long-range mesh networking hardware that delivers connectivity across remote environments.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Williams pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
Overwatch Bidco Pty Ltd, Five V Capital Pty Ltd and Five V Horizons
Pty Ltd applied for a notification waiver in respect of Overwatch
Bidco Pty Ltd’s proposed acquisition of the majority of the issued
shares in FTP Group Holdings Pty Ltd and its subsidiaries (the FTP
Group), as described in the transaction documents provided as part
of the application (the Acquisition).

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be
notified.
24 March 2026

The acquirer, Overwatch Bidco Pty Ltd, is indirectly wholly owned by
Horizons Fund and Frontier Fund, investment funds managed by Five
V Capital Pty Ltd (Five V Capital). Horizons Fund and Frontier Fund
pursue strategies of investing in private mid-market businesses. Five
V Capital is an Australian-incorporated private equity and venture
capital firm with headquarters in Australia and New Zealand. It
manages a number of Five V funds which have investments in a wide
range of businesses and industries.
The target, FTP Group, is based in Australia, Canada and New
Zealand and operates under the business names ‘FTP Solutions’,
‘Faarm’ and ‘Yarn Mesh’. FTP Solutions provides integrated network
and operational monitoring software to the global mining industry.
Faarm is a software platform that aggregates disparate farm data
into a single interface to enable farmers to monitor farms. Yarn
Mesh designs and manufactures long-range mesh networking
hardware that delivers connectivity across remote environments.
A connected entity of Five V Capital, Beacon TopCo Pty Ltd trading
as Digital Matter (Digital Matter), supplies IoT hardware solutions
and device management software that enable long-term asset
tracking, sensor monitoring and telematics applications. Digital
Matter’s solutions can be used in several settings, including in the
agricultural setting, for example for remote sensor monitoring,
environmental monitoring, and water monitoring.
Broadly, Digital Matter and FTP Group’s Faarm business (Faarm)
both supply solutions that facilitate remote monitoring for use in
agricultural settings. Digital Matter supplies hardware devices (with
some associated software such as device management software),
whereas Faarm offers a platform that integrates with third party
hardware.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian
Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered
the information provided with the notification waiver application and
had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition
and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).
Based on the information currently before it, the ACCC considers that
the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material lessening of
competition. In particular:
a. There is no horizontal overlap or vertical relationship
between FTP Group and Five V Capital and its connected
entities.
b. There are several other suppliers of agricultural monitoring
solutions and of IoT hardware solutions and device
management software in Australia.
c. Faarm has a relatively small presence in Australia.
d. While there is a potential complementary relationship
between Digital Matter and Faarm in the supply of
monitoring solutions for use in agricultural settings, there is
a low risk of concerning conglomerate effects resulting from
exclusionary conduct and/or bundling or tying of products
post-Acquisition.
The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the
Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds
determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.
While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely
to be met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to
arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required
to be notified.
The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the
object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting
competition.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notification waiver applications and to assessing competition
effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on
notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

merger_name: Brisbane Airport Corporation – Interest in land at Brisbane Airport

targets.name: GROWTHPOINT PROPERTIES AUSTRALIA LIMITED AS TRUSTEE FOR THE WHOLESALE INDUSTRIAL PROPERTY FUND

merger_description:
Brisbane Airport Corporation Pty Limited (**Brisbane Airport Corporation**) operates, manages, and develops Brisbane Airport. Brisbane Airport Corporation facilitates international and domestic travel, manages over 2,700 hectares of land, provides infrastructure (terminals, runways, roads), and facilitates commercial/retail/industrial activities through the leasing of the airport land.

Growthpoint Properties Australia Limited (**GOZ**) is an ASX-listed real estate investment trust, which carries on a business primarily engaged in leasing, buying and selling land and managing a portfolio of office, industrial and retail assets for third party wholesale syndicates and institutional investors. GOZ invests in Australian real estate and has a directly owned portfolio of office and industrial properties which it leases to commercial tenants. Growthpoint Properties Australia Limited as trustee of the Wholesale Industrial Property Fund (**Growthpoint**) holds a leasehold interest (as lessee) of the properties located at 3, 5 and 7A Viola Place, Brisbane Airport under the Head Leases from Brisbane Airport Corporation (as lessor).

Brisbane Airport Corporation has entered into a Contract for the Sale and Purchase of Commercial Real Estate to acquire from Growthpoint its interest as lessee under the Head Leases and as sublessor under the Subleases for lots located at 3, 5 and 7A Viola Place, Brisbane Airport, Queensland.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Woodward pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
Brisbane Airport Corporation Pty Limited (Brisbane Airport
Corporation) applied for a notification waiver in respect of its
proposed acquisition from Growthpoint Properties Australia Limited
as trustee of the Wholesale Industrial Property Fund (Growthpoint)
of Growthpoint’s interest as lessee under the Head Leases and as
sublessor under the Subleases for lots located at 3, 5 and 7A Viola
Place, Brisbane Airport, Queensland, as described in the transaction
documents provided as part of the application (the Acquisition).

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be
notified.
8 April 2026

The acquirer, Brisbane Airport Corporation, operates, manages, and
develops Brisbane Airport. Brisbane Airport Corporation facilitates
international and domestic travel, manages over 2,700 hectares of
land, provides infrastructure (terminals, runways, roads), and
facilitates commercial, retail and industrial activity through the
leasing of the airport land.
Growthpoint Properties Australia Limited (GOZ) is an ASX-listed real
estate investment trust, which carries on a business primarily
engaged in leasing, buying and selling land and managing a portfolio
of office, industrial and retail assets for third party wholesale
syndicates and institutional investors. GOZ invests in Australian real
estate and has a directly owned portfolio of office and industrial
properties which it leases to commercial tenants.
Growthpoint holds a leasehold interest (as lessee) of the properties
located at 3, 5 and 7A Viola Place, Brisbane Airport (the Property)
under the Head Leases from Brisbane Airport Corporation (as
lessor).
The Property would be an input into the operations of Brisbane
Airport Corporation.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian
Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered
the information provided with the notification waiver application and
certain publicly available information, and had regard to the factors
in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010
(Cth) (the Act).
Based on the information currently before it, the ACCC considers that
the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material lessening of
competition. In particular:
a. Brisbane Airport Corporation is the head lessor in respect of
the Property and will be acquiring interests in existing leases
b. there are alternative sites available to develop or lease at
Brisbane Airport, and in the wider Brisbane area, that are
held by third parties.
In these circumstances, the ACCC does not consider it necessary to
reach a concluded view on the likelihood of the notification
thresholds being met.
Given that material competition concerns are unlikely to arise, the
ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required to be
notified.
The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the
object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting
competition.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notification waiver applications and to assessing competition
effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on
notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

merger_description:
BGIS Pty Ltd (**BGIS**) proposes to acquire 100% of the issued shares in the Ascot Air Facilities Pty Ltd (**Ascot Air**) from its shareholders (the **Acquisition**).

BGIS is a national provider of integrated facilities management services and supplies heating, ventilation and air conditioning (**HVAC**) services largely as part of broader facilities management arrangements for portfolio customers, either through internal service capability or specialist subcontractors.

Ascot Air is a provider of commercial HVAC and mechanical services, supplying commercial HVAC design and construction, chilled water systems, refrigeration, ventilation ductwork, pipework and mechanical services, computer room air conditioning systems, building management systems and controls, drafting and design support and commissioning services.

Ascot Air provides services across South East Queensland, including Brisbane, Ipswich, Logan, Redland, Moreton Bay and the Gold Coast.

determination_table_content.details:
BGIS Pty Ltd (BGIS) applied for a notification waiver in respect of its
proposed acquisition of 100% of the share capital in Ascot Air
Facilities Pty Ltd (Ascot Air), as described in the transaction
documents provided as part of the application (the Acquisition).

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be
notified.
7 April 2026

The acquirer, BGIS, is a national provider of integrated facilities
management services and supplies heating, ventilation and air
conditioning (HVAC) services largely as part of broader facilities
management arrangements for portfolio customers, either through
internal service capability or specialist subcontractors.
The target, Ascot Air, is a provider of commercial HVAC and
mechanical services, supplying commercial HVAC design and
construction, chilled water systems, refrigeration, ventilation
ductwork, pipework and mechanical services, computer room air
conditioning systems, building management systems and controls,
drafting and design support and commissioning services. Ascot Air
provides services across South East Queensland, including Brisbane,
Ipswich, Logan, Redland, Moreton Bay and the Gold Coast.
Both parties supply commercial HVAC and related mechanical
services within South East Queensland.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian
Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered
the information provided with the notification waiver application and
certain publicly available information, and had regard to the factors
in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010
(Cth) (the Act).
Based on the information currently before it, the ACCC considers that
the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material lessening of
competition. In particular:
a. there is a small horizontal overlap between BGIS and Ascot
Air in the supply of commercial HVAC and related
mechanical services to areas within South East Queensland
b. while there is a potential vertical supply relationship between
the parties, there appears to be a low risk of foreclosure or
other concerning vertical effects resulting from exclusionary
conduct post-acquisition
c. there are alternative suppliers of commercial HVAC and
related mechanical services in areas within South East
Queensland.
The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the
Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds
determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.
While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely
to be met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to
arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required

merger_description:
Rosewood AG, LP, a Guernsey limited partnership (indirectly owned by the Eighth Cinven Fund) proposes to acquire voting rights in Collagen Holdings, LLC (trading as **Regenity**) (the **Acquisition**).

The Eighth Cinven Fund is indirectly managed and controlled by Cinven Limited. Cinven Limited has exclusive authority to make investment and management decisions for Eighth Cinven Fund.

Eighth Cinven Fund and Cinven Limited are part of Cinven. Cinven is a private equity firm engaged in the provision of investment management and investment advisory services to a number of investment funds. The controlled companies in Cinven’s portfolio of investments are active in a variety of sectors including business services, consumer, financial services, healthcare, industrials and technology, media and telecommunications.

The Acquisition will result in Cinven Limited and the existing investor, Linden Capital Partners, having joint control of Regenity.

Linden Capital Partners is a Chicago-based private equity firm focused exclusively on the healthcare industry.

Regenity is a global developer and manufacturer of bioresorbable technologies. The company is headquartered in Paramus, New Jersey.

merger_name: CVC Capital Partners – The animal nutrition and health division of the DSM-Firmenich Group

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Williams pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
CVC Capital Partners plc. (CVC Capital Partners) proposes to
acquire the animal nutrition and health division of the DSM-
Firmenich Group as described in the transaction documents
provided as part of the application (the Acquisition).
The Acquisition consists of:
• CVC Capital Partners to indirectly acquire, through Specialty
Bidco B.V., Speciality (U.S.) Bidco, Inc., Essential Bidco B.V.,
and Essential (U.S.) Bidco, Inc., an 80% shareholding and
sole control over the SpecialtyCo Business and EssentialCo
Business (which together comprise the animal nutrition and
health division), and
• the DSM-Firmenich Group to (indirectly through to be
incorporated entities) reinvest in each of the SpecialityCo
Business and the EssentialCo Business to retain a ~20%
non-controlling, minority stake in each.
Specialty Bidco B.V., Speciality (U.S.) Bidco, Inc., Essential Bidco
B.V., and Essential (U.S.) Bidco, Inc applied for a notification waiver
in respect of the Acquisition.

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be
notified.
7 April 2026

The acquirer, CVC Capital Partners, is a global alternative investment
manager focused on private equity, credit, infrastructure and
secondaries. CVC Capital Partners hold interests in companies
active in a variety of industries around the world, including in
financial services, chemicals, utilities, manufacturing, retailing and
distribution, primarily in Europe, the US and the Asia-Pacific region.
The target, the animal nutrition and health division of the DSM-
Firmenich Group, is a global provider of science-based animal
nutrition and health solutions. Its core business is the manufacture
and supply of animal nutrition ingredients, particularly vitamins and
carotenoids, for livestock and aquaculture feed. It is also involved in
the premix industry, which serve as a link from feed additives to final
feed composition in the animal nutrition value chain.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian
Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered
the information provided with the notification waiver and had regard
to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).
Based on the information provided in the application, the ACCC
considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material
lessening of competition. In particular, there is no horizontal overlap
between the products and services supplied by the animal nutrition
and health division of the DSM-Firmenich Group and CVC Capital
Partners controlled portfolio companies in Australia, or any vertical
relationships.
The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the
Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds
determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.
While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely
to be met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to
arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required
to be notified.
The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the
object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting
competition.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notification waiver applications and to assessing competition
effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on
notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

anzsic_codes.name: Engineering Design and Engineering Consulting Services

merger_description:
WSP Global Inc. (**WSP**) is a global engineering consulting services firm, which is listed on the Toronto Stock Exchange. In Australia, WSP operates through its wholly owned subsidiary, WSP Australia Pty Limited, and provides engineering services to customers across various industries (except where otherwise indicated, WSP and WSP Australia Pty Ltd are collectively referred to as WSP).

TRC Companies, Inc. (**TRC**) (referred to as the Target, together with its related subsidiaries forming the TRC group) is a US-based privately held engineering consulting and construction services firm which predominantly services clients in the US who own or operate large-scale infrastructure and land assets. TRC has no subsidiary, employees or other physical presence in Australia and has immaterial supplies to Australian customers from its offshore operations.

WSP has entered into an agreement to indirectly acquire 100% of the shares of TRC.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Chair Cass-Gottlieb pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
WSP Global Inc. (WSP) applied for a notification waiver in respect of
its proposed acquisition of 100% of the share capital in TRC
Companies, Inc. (TRC), as described in the transaction documents
provided as part of the application (the Acquisition).

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be
notified.
12 January 2026

The acquirer, WSP, is a global engineering consulting services firm
which provides services related to planning, engineering, quality
assurance and implementation of large engineering projects in the
construction, energy, transport, infrastructure, building, and natural
resource sectors in Australia.
The target, TRC, is an engineering consulting and construction
management firm that predominantly works with clients in the US
who own or operate large-scale infrastructure and land assets,
including energy providers, utility providers, transportation agencies,
government bodies, and industrial and commercial enterprises. TRC
provides limited services to customers outside of the US.
The parties both supply engineering consulting services, including for
the energy, transport and infrastructure sectors.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian
Competition and Consumer Commission (ACCC) has considered the
information provided with the notification waiver application and had
regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (Act), being:
(i) the object of the Act,
(ii) the interests of consumers,
(iii) the likelihood that, if the acquisition were put into effect,
the notification thresholds in section 51ABP(1) of the Act
would apply, and
(iv) the likelihood that the acquisition, if put into effect, would
in all the circumstances have the effect, or be likely to
have the effect, of substantially lessening competition in
any market.
Based on the information provided in the application, the ACCC
considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material
lessening of competition. In particular:
a. There is very limited horizontal overlap between WSP and
TRC in the supply of goods or services in Australia. The
information provided by WSP in its application indicates that
TRC currently has no presence in Australia and generates
negligible revenue from offshore sales to customers in
Australia.
b. The parties’ combined market share in the broader supply of
engineering consulting services in Australia is very low and
any aggregation in more specialised service offerings would
be minimal.
c. The parties are likely to continue to be constrained by
alternative global or national suppliers of engineering
consulting services in Australia, including design and project
management services in the energy, environment, transport
and infrastructure sectors.
The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the
Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds
determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.
While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely
to be met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to
arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required to
be notified.
The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the
object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting
competition.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notification waiver applications and to assessing competition effects
more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on notification
waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

merger_description:
IFS North America Inc. (**IFS North America**), via its wholly owned subsidiary incorporated for the purposes of the transaction, Fjord Merger Sub, Inc. (**Merger Sub**), proposes to acquire 100% of the issued and outstanding shares of Softeon, Inc. (**Softeon**) through a reverse triangular merger where:

(a) Merger Sub will merge with and into Softeon; and

(b) Merger Sub will then cease, and Softeon will be the surviving company.

As a result, Softeon will become a wholly owned subsidiary of IFS North America.

IFS North America is owned by Industrial and Financial Systems Aktiebolag, which is headquartered in Linköping, Sweden (together, IFS). IFS develops and commercialises business software for customers that manufacture and distribute goods, maintain assets and manage services-focused operations. Its technology enables manufacturing businesses to maintain complex assets and manage service-focused operations to enhance productivity, efficiency and sustainability.

Softeon is headquartered in Reston, VA. and supplies a supply chain fulfilment software platform for warehouses. Softeon operates in the warehouse management systems segment and provides a suite of supply chain software solutions on a single, integrated services-based technology platform that offers the following cloud-based software capabilities: warehouse management system, warehouse execution system and distributed order management solutions.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by a division of the Commission constituted by a direction issued pursuant to section 19 of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
IFS North America Inc. (IFS North America) applied for a notification
waiver in respect of its proposed acquisition, via its subsidiary Fjord
Merger Sub Inc (Merger Sub), of 100% of the share capital in Softeon
Inc. (Softeon), as described in the transaction documents provided
as part of the application (the Acquisition).

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be
notified.
27 January 2026

The acquirer, IFS North America, develops and commercialises
business software for customers that manufacture and distribute
goods, maintain assets and manage services-focused operations.
The software includes functionalities that use artificial intelligence to
support productivity, efficiency, and sustainable objectives.
IFS North America is owned by Industrial and Financial Systems
Aktiebolag, which is headquartered in Linköping, Sweden (together,
IFS). IFS is jointly controlled by EQT IX, an investment fund managed
by EQT Fund Management S.à r.l. (a global investment organisation),
and by Hg Pooled Management Limited, an investor in software and
services businesses, with a focus on Europe and the United States.
The target, Softeon, is a global software provider of warehouse
management systems and innovative supply chain solutions. Softeon
is headquartered in Reston, Virginia, USA and has a very limited
presence in Australia.
Both parties supply enterprise application software, however IFS’s
Australian business mainly supplies enterprise resource planning
services, whereas Softeon specialises in warehouse management
and supply chain management solutions. These services are largely
complementary.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian
Competition and Consumer Commission (ACCC) has considered the
information provided with the notification waiver application and had
regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (Act).
Based on the information provided in the application, the ACCC
considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material
lessening of competition. In particular:
a. Softeon currently has a very small presence in Australia.
b. There is a small horizontal overlap between IFS and Softeon
in the supply of enterprise application software in Australia,
and no overlap in the supply of enterprise resource planning
and supply chain management services.
c. The parties’ combined shares in the supply of enterprise
application software in Australia are small, and they are likely
to continue to be constrained by several competitors.
d. There is a low risk of concerning conglomerate effects
resulting from bundling or tying of products post-merger.
The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the
Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds
determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.
While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely
to be met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to
arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required to
be notified.
The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the
object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting
competition.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notification waiver applications and to assessing competition effects
more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on notification
waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

anzsic_codes.name: Computer System Design and Related Services

merger_description:
Salesforce, Inc (**Salesforce**) is a global supplier of customer relationship management (**CRM**) software and other software-as-a-service solutions that enable companies to manage and improve their relationships with customers. Salesforce is a publicly listed company on the New York Stock Exchange and is headquartered in San Francisco, California.

Qualified.com, Inc (**Qualified**), founded in 2018 and headquartered in San Francisco, California, is a provider of an agentic marketing product. Qualified offers its products worldwide, including in Australia.

Both Salesforce and Qualified supply conversation automation solutions for business-to-business (**B2B**) uses, which integrate with Salesforce’s CRM software. Conversation automation solutions for B2B uses includes tools, such as virtual assistants, artificial intelligence (**AI**) agents, and chatbots, that support functions such as outbound and inbound sales prospecting for lead generation, customer qualification, customer research, meeting scheduling, and personalised product recommendations.

Salesforce proposes to acquire by merger all of the outstanding voting securities of Qualified.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by a division of the Commission constituted by a direction issued pursuant to section 19 of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
Salesforce, Inc (Salesforce) applied for a notification waiver in
respect of its proposed acquisition of the outstanding voting
securities of Qualified.com, Inc (Qualified), as described in the
transaction documents provided as part of the application (the
Acquisition).

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABV(1)(b) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) not to make the notification waiver
determination applied for.
20 January 2026

The acquirer, Salesforce, is a global supplier of customer
relationship management (CRM) software and other software-as-a-
service solutions that enable companies to manage and improve
their relationship with customers. Salesforce is a publicly listed
company on the New York Stock Exchange and is headquartered in
San Francisco, California. Salesforce offers a suite of artificial
intelligence (AI) agents, which are integrated with its core CRM
offerings and automate tasks related to sales, service, marketing
and commerce, such as personalised engagement with potential and
existing customers and scheduling meetings.
The target, Qualified, is a supplier of an AI-based sales development
representative (SDR) agent, known as Piper, which integrates with
Salesforce’s CRM platform. Piper is an agentic marketing product
that is focused on engaging customers through a company’s website
and converting them into buyers. Qualified is headquartered in San
Francisco, California, and offers its products worldwide, including in
Australia.
Salesforce and Qualified overlap in Australia in the supply of
conversation automation solutions for business-to-business (B2B)
uses, such as virtual assistants, AI agents or chatbots, to support
sales and marketing teams to engage with customers.
In addition to the horizontal overlap, there is a vertical relationship
between Qualified’s product Piper and Salesforce’s CRM platform.
Piper uses Salesforce’s CRM data to inform its agent’s interactions
and records information about customer engagement in Salesforce’s
CRM platform.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian
Competition and Consumer Commission (ACCC) has considered the
information provided with the notification waiver application, public
information and other industry information in the possession of the
ACCC from its consideration of prior matters, and had regard to the
factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition and Consumer Act
2010 (Cth) (Act), being:
(i) the object of the Act,
(ii) the interests of consumers,
(iii) the likelihood that, if the acquisition were put into effect, the
notification thresholds in section 51ABP(1) of the Act would
apply, and
(iv) the likelihood that the acquisition, if put into effect, would in
all the circumstances have the effect, or be likely to have the
effect, of substantially lessening competition in any market.
Based on the information provided in the application, including
Salesforce’s revenue in Australia and the transaction value, the
ACCC considers that, if the Acquisition were put into effect, the
notification thresholds would be met.
The ACCC also considers that the Acquisition may raise competition
issues. In particular:
a. Salesforce is the leading provider of CRM software to
businesses in Australia and is significantly larger than the
next largest supplier.
b. The parties operate in complex and dynamic markets,
including the provision of agentic AI.
c. There is a vertical relationship between the products
supplied by Salesforce and Qualified. The extent of the
parties’ ability and/or incentive to foreclose rivals to
Qualified or Salesforce warrants further consideration to be
able to form a conclusive view on the competition impacts
of the Acquisition, despite the relatively small presence of
Qualified in Australia at present.
The ACCC considers that these potential competition issues warrant
consideration through the notification process, including via
consultation with third parties.
Given the above, the ACCC has determined not to make the
notification waiver determination applied for.
The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the
object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting
competition.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notification waiver applications and to assessing competition
effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on
notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

merger_name: L Catterton Management – Ex Nihilo Group

anzsic_codes.name: Pharmaceutical, Cosmetic and Toiletry Goods Retailing

merger_description:
L Catterton Management Limited, together with its controlled funds and affiliates (LCAT), proposes to acquire ~45% of the issued share capital of Ex Nihilo Group (Ex Nihilo), through LCAT’s controlled entity, Saphir Holdings SAS (Saphir).

LCAT is a consumer-focused private equity firm based in the United States with assets under management globally.

Ex Nihilo is a luxury fragrance house based in France specialising in the creation, production, and wholesale and retail distribution of high-end perfumes and related products. Ex Nihilo was launched in 2013 and has retailers operating worldwide.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Williams pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
L Catterton Management Limited (LCAT) applied for a notification
waiver in respect of its proposed acquisition, through its controlled
entity Saphir Holdings SAS (Saphir) and together with its controlled
funds and affiliates, of ~45% of the issued share capital in Ex Nihilo
Group (Ex Nihilo), as described in the transaction documents
provided as part of the application (the Acquisition).

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be
notified.
4 February 2026

LCAT is a consumer-focused private equity firm based in the United
States with assets under management globally. LCAT has 12
controlled investments that are active in the wholesale and retail
supply of perfumes. Three of these investments (Vyrao, A.P.C. and
Merit) have activities in Australia. The acquirer, Saphir, is a controlled
entity of LCAT.
The target, Ex Nihilo, is a luxury fragrance house based in France
specialising in the creation, production, and wholesale and retail
distribution of high-end perfumes and related products. Ex Nihilo
was launched in 2013 and has retailers operating worldwide.
LCAT’s controlled investments and Ex Nihilo overlap in the wholesale
and retail supply of perfumes in Australia.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian
Competition and Consumer Commission (ACCC) has considered the
information provided with the notification waiver application and had
regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (Act).
Based on the information provided in the application, the ACCC
considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material
lessening of competition. In particular:
a. Ex Nihilo has a small presence in Australia.
b. The horizontal overlap between entities controlled by LCAT
and Ex Nihilo in the wholesale and retail supply of perfumes
in Australia appears to be small.
c. The parties’ combined share of supply in the wholesale and
retail supply of perfumes is low.
d. There are numerous alternative wholesale and retail
suppliers of perfumes in Australia.
The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the
Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds
determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.
While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely
to be met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to
arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required
to be notified.
The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the
object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting
competition.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notification waiver applications and to assessing competition
effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on
notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

merger_description:
Superloop Limited (ASX: SLC) (**Superloop**) proposes to acquire 100% of the shares in Lynham Networks Pty. Ltd. (**Lynham Networks**).

Superloop’s core business is retail broadband services, operating as a retail service provider (**RSP**) supplying broadband services to end users, primarily using the NBN network as well as a private fibre-to-the-premises (**FTTP**) network. Superloop also has a number of wholesale FTTP networks under its subsidiaries, VostroNet and Frontier, which are statutory infrastructure providers (**SIP**) that build and operate wholesale access FTTP broadband networks for declared service areas. Superloop provides retail fixed broadband services on its FTTP network as well as wholesaling FTTP services to other RSPs on a non-discriminatory basis.

Lynham Network is a FTTP network operator and SIP supplying wholesale, open access network services that enable RSPs to sell fixed broadband services to end customers. Its core business involves building and operating fibre networks primarily for new residential and mixed-use developments. Lynham Network also has a retail arm, Lightning Broadband, which sells broadband services to residential and business customers but only over the Lynham FTTP network. Lynham does not offer broadband services over the NBN network.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Williams pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
Superloop Limited (Superloop) applied for a notification waiver in
respect of its proposed acquisition of 100% of the share capital in
Lynham Networks Pty. Ltd. (Lynham Networks), as described in the

Acquisition).

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be
notified.
24 February 2026

The acquirer, Superloop, is primarily a retail broadband services
business, operating as a retail service provider supplying broadband
services to end users, primarily using the NBN network as well as a
private fibre-to-the-premises (FTTP) network. Superloop also has a
number of wholesale FTTP networks under its subsidiaries,
VostroNet and Frontier, which are statutory infrastructure providers
(SIP) that build and operate wholesale access FTTP broadband
networks for declared service areas. Superloop provides retail fixed
broadband services on its FTTP network as well as wholesaling
FTTP services to other retail service providers on a non-
discriminatory basis.
The target, Lynham Networks, is primarily a FTTP operator that
builds and operates fibre networks primarily for new residential and
mixed-use developments. Lynham Networks is a SIP supplying
wholesale, open access network services that enable retail service
providers to sell fixed broadband services to end customers. Lynham
Networks also has a retail arm, Lightning Broadband, which sells
broadband services to residential and business customers but only
over the Lynham Networks FTTP network. Lynham Networks does
not offer broadband services over the NBN network.
Both Superloop and Lynham Networks operate in the
telecommunications and internet industry providing fixed retail and
wholesale broadband services.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian
Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered
the information provided with the notification waiver application and
had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition
and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).
Based on the information provided in the application, the ACCC
considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material
lessening of competition. In particular:
a. there is a small horizontal overlap between Superloop and
Lynham Networks in the supply of retail and wholesale fixed
broadband services in Australia
b. the presence of alternative providers of retail and wholesale
fixed broadband services in Australia.
The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the
Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds

to be notified
The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the
object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting
competition.

notification waiver applications and to assessing competition
effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on
notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

merger_name: VFMC – CorVal Land and Operator Trusts

anzsic_codes.name: Other Auxiliary Finance and Investment Services

merger_description:
Victorian Funds Management Corporation (**VFMC**), as investment manager of several investment vehicles (**VFMC Clients**), proposes for the VFMC Clients to subscribe to units in two stapled trusts of which CorVal Partners Limited acts as trustee, comprising RF CorVal MHE Land Trust (**Land Trust**) and RF CorVal MHE Operator Trust (**Operator Trust**) (together, **MHE Fund**).

MHE Fund is a stapled structure pursuant to which the Land Trust holds properties and leases them to the Operator Trust. MHE Fund’s downstream co-venture, also comprised of a stapled land trust and operator trust, develops and manages residential property.

The VFMC Clients involved in the acquisition are the Emergency Services Superannuation Board (as trustee for Emergency Services Superannuation Scheme), the Transport Accident Commission, the Victorian Managed Insurance Authority, the Victorian WorkCover Authority, and the VFMC Investment Fund (of which VFMC is the trustee).

Following completion of the transaction, State Street Australia Ltd, acting as custodian for each of the VFMC Clients, will hold minority interests in the relevant MHE Fund entities.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Williams pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
Victorian Funds Management Corporation (VFMC), as investment
manager of the following investment vehicles:
• the Emergency Services Superannuation Board (as trustee
for Emergency Services Superannuation Scheme)
• the Transport Accident Commission
• the Victorian Managed Insurance Authority
• the Victorian WorkCover Authority, and
• the VFMC Investment Fund (of which VFMC is the trustee)
(together, the VFMC Clients),
proposes that the VFMC Clients subscribe to units in two stapled
trusts of which CorVal Partners Limited acts as trustee, comprising
RF CorVal MHE Land Trust (Land Trust) and RF CorVal MHE
Operator Trust (Operator Trust) (together, the MHE Fund), as
described in the transaction documents provided as part of the
application (the Acquisition).
As part of the Acquisition, State Street Australia Ltd (State Street),
acting as custodian for each of the VFMC Clients, will hold minority
interests in the relevant MHE Fund entities.
A notification waiver application in respect of the Acquisition was
made by VFMC, the Emergency Service Superannuation Board, the
Transport Accident Commission, the Victorian Managed Insurance
Authority and the Victorian WorkCover Authority.

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be
notified.
26 March 2026

VFMC is a statutory authority established under the Victorian Funds
Management Corporation Act 1994 (Vic) to provide investment and
funds management services to public sector insurance and
superannuation agencies.
VFMC acts as investment manager for a number of clients and
invests across a range of asset classes, including Australian and
international equities, infrastructure and property, inflation linked
bonds, diversified fixed interest, non-traditional strategies (hedge
funds, private credit, insurance and others) and cash.
State Street, the entity that will hold the units in the relevant MHE
Fund entities as a custodian for each of the VFMC Clients, provides
financial solutions to institutional investors through investment
servicing and management, and market and data intelligence.
The target, the MHE Fund, is active in the property and management
industry. The MHE Fund is a stapled structure pursuant to which the
Land Trust holds properties and leases them to the Operator Trust.
MHE Fund’s downstream co-venture, also comprised of a stapled
land trust and operator trust, develops and manages residential
property. CorVal Partners Limited acts as trustee of the MHE Fund.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian
Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered
the information provided with the notification waiver application and
had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition
and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).
Based on the information provided in the application, the ACCC
considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material
lessening of competition. In particular:
a. there is no horizontal overlap or vertical relationship
between the current investments of VFMC and/or the VFMC
Clients and the activities of the MHE Fund
b. there are alternative developers and managers of residential
property and manufactured housing estates in Australia.
The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the
Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds
determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.
While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely
to be met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to
arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required
to be notified.
The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the
object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting
competition.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notification waiver applications and to assessing competition
effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on
notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

Other Auxiliary Finance and Investment Services

merger_description:
On 27 February 2026, Rocket Purchaser, L.L.C. (an affiliate of various funds advised and/or managed by affiliates of Blackstone, Inc. (**Blackstone**)) entered into an Agreement and Plan of Merger to acquire all the issued shares in Advanced Cooling Technologies, Inc. (**ACT**).

ACT is a US-based engineering and manufacturing company specialising in advanced thermal management and cooling solutions for a range of industries. ACT provides passive and active heat transfer technologies, including heat pipes, two-phase and pumped liquid cooling loops, thermal storage devices, and custom engineered systems for applications in aerospace, defence, spacecraft, electronics, data centres, HVAC, medical devices, energy recovery and industrial markets.

Blackstone is a US-based investment management firm, incorporated under the laws of Delaware, United States.

merger_name: Austral Resources Australia – Lady Loretta mine

anzsic_codes.name: Copper, Silver, Lead and Zinc Smelting and Refining

merger_description:
On 16 February 2026, Austral Resources Australia Ltd (**Austral**) entered into an agreement with Noranda Pacific No. 2 Pty Ltd (**NP2**) and Mount Isa Mines Limited (**MIM**) (both indirect subsidiaries of Glencore plc) to acquire:

* 100% of the shares in Noranda Pacific Pty Ltd (**NPPL**), the owner and operator of the Lady Loretta zinc mine in Queensland, from NP2 and MIM; and
* the exploration permits associated with the Lady Loretta mine from MIM, (together, the **Acquisition**).

The Lady Loretta mine ceased production in 2025.

Austral is an Australian-based copper producer headquartered in Queensland. Austral owns and operates a number of copper mines in Australia, including the Lady Annie copper mine which is located 16km south of the Lady Loretta mine.

NPPL, NP2 and MIM are indirect wholly-owned subsidiaries of Glencore plc, a multi-national commodity trading and mining company headquartered in Switzerland.

Following completion, Austral will assume the progressive rehabilitation obligations of the Lady Loretta mine, and will enter into an offtake agreement with Glencore in respect of future copper products derived from the Lady Loretta tenements.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Williams pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
Austral Resources Australia Limited (Austral) applied for a
notification waiver in respect of its proposed acquisition of:
• 100% of the shares in Noranda Pacific Pty Ltd (NPPL), the
owner and operator of the Lady Loretta zinc mine in
Queensland, from Noranda Pacific No. 2 Pty Ltd (NP2) and
Mount Isa Mines Limited (MIM); and
• the exploration permits associated with the Lady Loretta
mine from MIM,
as described in the transaction documents provided as part of the
application (the Acquisition).

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be
notified.
7 April 2026

The acquirer, Austral, is an Australian-based copper producer
headquartered in Queensland. Austral owns and operates a number
of copper mines in Australia, including the Lady Annie copper mine
which is located 16km south of the Lady Loretta mine.
The Lady Loretta mine ceased production in 2025. Prior to its
closure, the mine produced zinc concentrate (with lead and silver
concentrate by-products), 100% of which was supplied to related
entities of NPPL for processing in Mount Isa.
NPPL, NP2 and MIM are indirect wholly-owned subsidiaries of
Glencore plc, a multi-national commodity trading and mining
company headquartered in Switzerland.
Following completion, Austral will assume the progressive
rehabilitation obligations of the Lady Loretta mine, and will enter into
an offtake agreement with Glencore in respect of future copper
products derived from the Lady Loretta tenements.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian
Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered
the information provided with the notification waiver application and
certain publicly available information, and had regard to the factors
in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010
(Cth) (the Act).
Based on the information before it, the ACCC considers that the
Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material lessening of
competition. In particular:
a. Austral’s share of the actual and expected production and
supply of copper concentrate and zinc concentrate in
Australia and globally is expected to be low
b. there are several alternative producers and suppliers of
copper concentrate and zinc concentrate in Australia and
globally.
In these circumstances, the ACCC does not consider it necessary to
reach a concluded view on the likelihood of the notification
thresholds being met.
Given that material competition concerns are unlikely to arise, the
ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required to be
notified.
The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the
object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting
competition.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notification waiver applications and to assessing competition
effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on
notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

merger_name: Star Hotels Group – Yandina Hotel, QLD

targets.name: The Trustee for Kingston Family Trust

merger_description:
Star Hotels is an independently owned and operated pub and liquor retail group, primarily operating pubs along the eastern seaboard of QLD as well in South Australia. It currently operates over 50 venues in QLD, including two pubs in the Sunshine Coast area.

The Yandina Hotel is owned by the Trustee for Kingston Family Trust (trading as Yandina Hotel), a single-site operator. The Yandina Hotel offers dining, accommodation, gaming and a bottle shop.

Under the Acquisition, Star Hotels will acquire both the freehold land and the business assets of the Yandina Hotel.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Philip Williams pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
Hotel Maroochydore Pty Ltd and SCHF Pty Ltd, subsidiaries of Star
Consolidated Holdings Ptd Ltd (Star Hotels) applied for a
notification waiver in respect of a proposed acquisition of the
business and assets of the Yandina Hotel in Yandina, Qld, as
described in the transaction documents provided as part of the
application (the Acquisition).

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be
notified.
2 February 2026

The acquirer, Star Hotels, is an independently owned and operated
pub and liquor retail group, primarily operating over 50 pubs along
the eastern seaboard of QLD and four in SA. It operates multiple
licensed venues, accommodation, liquor retail outfits under brands
such as Star Liquor, Sense of Taste and Bob’s Bulk Booze. It
currently operates two pubs in the Sunshine Coast area.
The target, Yandina Hotel, is a hotel in Yandina, QLD that offers
dining, accommodation, gaming and a bottle shop. It is currently
owned and operated by the Trustee for Kingston Family Trust
(trading as Yandina Hotel), a single-site operator.
Star Hotels and the Yandina Hotel overlap in the supply of dining,
accommodation, gaming and liquor in the Sunshine Coast area.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian
Competition and Consumer Commission (ACCC) has considered the
information provided by the applicant, including in the notification
waiver application, and had regard to the factors in section
51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (Act).
Based on the information provided by the applicant, the ACCC
considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material
lessening of competition. In particular:
a. while there is horizontal overlap between Star Hotels and the
Yandina Hotel, concentration and the combined share of the
parties in the supply of accommodation, dining, functions,
gaming and liquor services in the broader Sunshine Coast
area is low
b. the presence of alternative suppliers of accommodation,
dining, functions, gaming and liquor in the broader Sunshine
Coast and Noosa area.
The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the
Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds
determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.
While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely
to be met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to
arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required
to be notified.
The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the
object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting
competition.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notification waiver applications and to assessing competition
effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on
notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

companies active in a variety of industries around the world,
including in financial services, chemicals, utilities, manufacturing,
retailing and distribution, primarily in Europe, the US and the Asia-
Pacific region.
The target, Smiths Detection Group, is wholly owned and controlled
by Smiths Group International Holdings Limited. Smiths Detection
Group is a global provider of advanced security-screening and threat-
detection technologies used in aviation, ports and borders, urban
security, defence and critical infrastructure. This includes CT and X-
ray scanners, explosive and chemical trace detection devices,
among other technologies. The target also supplies software,
installation and commissioning, training, spare parts, repairs,
upgrades and long-term service contracts.
The application states that the Acquisition does not give rise to any
horizontal overlap or vertical relationship between the products and
services supplied by Smiths Detection Group and CVC Capital
Partners controlled portfolio companies in Australia.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian
Competition and Consumer Commission (ACCC) has considered the
information provided with the notification waiver application and had
regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (Act).
Based on the information provided in the application, the ACCC
considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material
lessening of competition. In particular, there is no horizontal overlap
or vertical relationship between CVC Capital Partners and Smiths
Detection Group in the supply of security screening and threat
detection technologies in Australia.
The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the
Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds
determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.
While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely
to be met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to
arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required
to be notified.
The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the
object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting
competition.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notification waiver applications and to assessing competition
effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on
notification waivers and merger assessment guidelines.

merger_name: Vets Central – Gawler Animal Hospital and Animalia Vet Clinic
merger_description:
Vets Central Holdings Pty Ltd (together, with its subsidiaries, **Vets Central**), proposes to acquire 100% of the business assets (both tangible and intangible) of Gawler Animal Hospital and Animalia Vet Clinic (the Targets) from Gawler Animal Hospital Pty Ltd and GAH 2.0 Pty Ltd, respectively.

Vets Central is an Australian-owned veterinary group that owns and operates 78 first opinion veterinary practices across Australia and New Zealand. In Australia, Vets Central’s practices provide core and ancillary veterinary services, for household pets as well as livestock, and other exotic species. The services primarily include routine, planned, and emergency veterinary services (excluding after-hours services).

The Targets are jointly owned, first opinion veterinary practices that provide routine, planned and emergency (excluding after-hours) veterinary services in the Gawler and Evanston areas of South Australia.

Vet Central and the Targets both provide a range of veterinary services for household and exotic pets in their respective local areas.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Philip Williams pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
Vets Central Holdings Pty Ltd (together, with its subsidiaries, Vets
Central) applied for a notification waiver in respect of its proposed
acquisition of 100% of the assets of Gawler Animal Hospital and
Animalia Vet Clinic as described in the transaction documents
provided as part of the application (the Acquisition).

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be
notified.
12 February 2026

The acquirer, Vets Central, is an Australian-owned veterinary group
that owns and operates 78 first opinion veterinary practices across
Australia and New Zealand. In Australia, Vets Central’s practices
provide core and ancillary veterinary services, for household pets as
well as livestock, and other exotic species. The services primarily
include routine, planned, and emergency veterinary services
(excluding after-hours services).
The targets, Gawler Animal Hospital and Animalia Vet Clinic, are
jointly owned, full-service veterinary practices in the Gawler and
Evanston areas of South Australia. Both businesses also provide a
range of first opinion core and ancillary veterinary services for
household pets and exotic pets in their respective local areas. The
Gawler Animal Hospital also provides behaviour classes and
complicated surgeries.
Vets Central operates 7 veterinary practices in South Australia, the
closest being over 30 km away from the targets.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian
Competition and Consumer Commission (ACCC) has considered the

Based on the information provided in the application the ACCC
considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material
lessening of competition. In particular:
a. There appears to be limited or no local geographic horizontal
overlap between Vets Central and the Gawler Animal
Hospital and Animalia Vet Clinic in the supply of veterinary
services in the Gawler and Evanston areas of South
Australia.
b. The parties’ combined share of supply of veterinary services
in South Australia and Australia is not high and there are
alternative suppliers in the Gawler and Evanston areas of

The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the

merger_name: Permira and Warburg Pincus - Clearwater Analytics

anzsic_codes.name: Data Processing and Web Hosting Services

merger_description:
Permira is a Guernsey registered private equity business engaged, through its subsidiaries and affiliates, in the provision of investment management services to a number of investment funds. Permira ultimately controls a number of private equity funds, which include portfolio companies active across the consumer, services, healthcare and technology sectors.

Warburg Pincus is private equity firm headquartered in New York, USA. Warburg Pincus’ portfolio companies are active in a variety of sectors, including consumer, energy, financial services, healthcare, industrial and business services and technology, media and telecommunications. In Australia, Warburg Pincus’ portfolio companies are primarily active in software and financial sectors.

Clearwater Analytics is a software-as-a-service company, headquartered in Boise, Idaho, USA, that provides automated, cloud-based software solutions for investors to manage investment data. Clearwater Analytics provides accounting and reporting, data, advanced analytics, risk and performance and portfolio and order management capabilities for a range of investor types across both public and private markets.

Clearwater Analytics’ platform services the following clients: asset managers, banks, corporates, energy (e.g., commodity trading), hedge funds, insurance companies, pensions and endowments, and the public sector (i.e., government entities managing public funds).

The Acquisition concerns the proposed acquisition of joint control over Clearwater Analytics by funds managed and advised by subsidiaries or affiliates of Permira and Warburg Pincus by way of purchase of shares.

variety of sectors, including consumer, energy, financial services,

and telecommunications. In Australia, Warburg Pincus LLC’s

Clearwater Analytics provides accounting and reporting, data,
advanced analytics, risk and performance and portfolio and order
management capabilities for a range of investor types across both
public and private markets.
Clearwater Analytics’ platform services the following clients: asset
managers, banks, corporates, energy (e.g., commodity trading),
hedge funds, insurance companies, pensions and endowments, and
the public sector (i.e., government entities managing public funds).

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian
Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered
the information provided with the notification waiver application and
had regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition
and Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act).
Based on the information currently before it, the ACCC considers that

While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely

merger_name: Element Zero's L701 – Option to lease DevelopmentWA industrial land at Boodarie Strategic Industrial Area
merger_description:
L701 Pty Ltd proposes to enter into an option to lease agreement with the Western Australian Land Authority (trading as **DevelopmentWA**) over part of Lot 701 within the Boodarie Strategic Industrial Area in Western Australia. The option provides L701 Pty Ltd with the right to lease the land following completion of development milestones.

L701 Pty Ltd is a subsidiary of Element Zero Pty Ltd (**Element Zero**), an Australian company developing technology and projects for the production of low-emissions mineral products, including green iron. Element Zero develops technology and projects for the production of low-carbon mineral products and intends to develop a commercial-scale green iron production facility.

DevelopmentWA is the Western Australian Government’s land and development agency responsible for facilitating industrial land development. DevelopmentWA develops and manages industrial land and infrastructure in Western Australia, including within the Boodarie Strategic Industrial Area.

If the option is exercised, the parties intend to enter into a long-term industrial ground lease to enable development and operation of a green iron production facility.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Williams pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
L701 Pty Ltd applied for a notification waiver in respect of its
proposal to enter into an option to lease agreement with the
Western Australian Land Authority (trading as DevelopmentWA) over
part of Lot 701 within the Boodarie Strategic Industrial Area in
Western Australia, as described in the transaction documents
provided as part of the application (the Acquisition). The option
provides L701 Pty Ltd with the right to lease the land following
completion of development milestones.

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be
notified.
7 April 2026

The acquirer, L701 Pty Ltd, is a subsidiary of Element Zero Pty Ltd
(Element Zero), an Australian company developing technology and
projects for the production of low-emissions mineral products,
including green iron. Element Zero develops technology and projects
for the production of low-carbon mineral products and intends to
develop a commercial-scale green iron production facility.
DevelopmentWA is the Western Australian Government’s land and
development agency responsible for facilitating industrial land
development. DevelopmentWA develops and manages industrial
land and infrastructure in Western Australia, including within the
Boodarie Strategic Industrial Area.
If the option for lease is exercised, the parties intend to enter into a
long-term industrial ground lease to enable development and
operation of a green iron production facility.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian
Competition and Consumer Commission (the ACCC) has considered
the information provided with the notification waiver application and
certain publicly available information, and had regard to the factors
in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition and Consumer Act 2010
(Cth) (the Act).
Based on the information currently before it, the ACCC considers that
the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material lessening of
competition. In particular:
a. there is no horizontal competitive overlap
b. there appear to be alternative sites available for Element
Zero’s competitors
c. the Acquisition does not appear likely to otherwise limit or
prevent competition from rivals.
In these circumstances, the ACCC does not consider it necessary to
reach a concluded view on the likelihood of the notification
thresholds being met.
Given that material competition concerns are unlikely to arise, the
ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required to be
notified.
The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the
object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting
competition.

effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on

merger_description:
Clifford Hallam Healthcare Pty Limited, a wholly-owned subsidiary of Paragon Care Limited (**Paragon Care**), proposes to acquire the Presidental business (**Presidental**) through an Asset Purchase Agreement from Presidental Pty Limited and Rockpac South Pacific Pty Ltd.

Paragon Care is a publicly traded company listed on the Australian Stock Exchange. It supplies pharmaceuticals, medical equipment and devices as well as medical and specialist dental consumables to the healthcare market in Australia, New Zealand and Asia. Paragon Care established its dedicated dental consumables business in May 2025.

Presidental is an Australian-owned business focusing on the supply of dental equipment and maintenance and repair services for dental equipment to dental clinics and surgeries in New South Wales, Brisbane and parts of regional Queensland, Victoria and Tasmania. Presidental also supplies a small volume of dental consumables associated with the dental equipment it supplies, as well as some steriliser machines and associated consumables to medical practices.

events.title: Notification waiver determination published
events.determination_commission_division: Determination made by Commissioner Williams pursuant to a delegation under section 25(1) of the Act

determination_table_content.details:
Clifford Hallam Healthcare Pty Limited, a wholly owned subsidiary of
Paragon Care Limited (Paragon Care), applied for a notification
waiver in respect of its proposed acquisition of the assets of
Presidental Pty Limited and Rockpac South Pacific Pty Ltd
(Presidental), as described in the transaction documents provided
as part of the application (the Acquisition).

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABV(1)(a) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition is not required to be
notified.
16 February 2026

Paragon Care is a publicly traded company listed on the Australian
Stock Exchange. It supplies pharmaceuticals, medical equipment
and devices as well as medical and specialist dental consumables to
the healthcare market in Australia, New Zealand and Asia. The
acquirer, Clifford Hallam Healthcare Pty Limited, is a wholly owned
subsidiary of Paragon Care.
The target, Presidental, is an Australian-owned business focusing on
the supply of dental equipment and maintenance and repair services
for dental equipment to dental clinics and surgeries in New South
Wales, Queensland, Victoria and Tasmania. Presidental also supplies
some dental consumables associated with the dental equipment it
supplies, as well as some steriliser machines and associated
consumables to medical practices.
The parties both supply dental consumables to dental surgeries and
clinics. The application states that dental consumables cover a
broad range of products that are typically used on a single use or
limited use basis in the course of delivering dental treatment and
maintaining clinical operations, such as face masks, fillings and
dental instruments.

In making this notification waiver determination, the Australian
Competition and Consumer Commission (ACCC) has considered the
information provided with the notification waiver application and had
regard to the factors in section 51ABV(2)(b) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (Act).
Based on the information provided by the applicant, the ACCC
considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to give rise to any material
lessening of competition. In particular:
a. The horizontal overlap between the parties in the supply of
dental consumables is small.

b. The parties’ combined share in the supply of dental
consumables is low.
c. There are a number of alternative suppliers of dental
consumables in Australia.
The ACCC has also had regard to the likelihood that, if the
Acquisition were put into effect, the notification thresholds
determined under section 51ABP(1) of the Act would apply.
While the ACCC considers that the notification thresholds are likely
to be met, given that material competition concerns are unlikely to
arise, the ACCC has determined that the Acquisition is not required
to be notified.
The ACCC considers that the determination is consistent with the
object of the Act and the interests of consumers in promoting

effects more generally, see the ACCC’s interim guidance on

merger_description:
Henkel AG & Co. KGaA, through its subsidiary Henkel Global Supply Chain B.V. (**Henkel**), proposes to acquire all the shares in and sole control of Stahl Parent B.V. (together with subsidiaries, **Stahl**) from Stahl Group S.A., which is indirectly controlled by affiliates of Wendel S.E. (**Wendel**).

Wendel is headquartered in Paris, France, and is a private assets-focused investment firm listed at the Euronext Paris stock exchange.

Henkel is a German public company listed on the Frankfurt Stock Exchange, headquartered in Düsseldorf. It is active worldwide through its Adhesive Technologies business and Consumer Brands business.

Henkel’s Adhesive Technologies business is a producer of adhesives, sealants and functional coatings for industrial customers, consumers and craftsmen. Its Adhesive Technologies brands available in Australia include Loctite, Teroson, Bonderite, Clingtape, Technomelt, Aquence and Pritt. Henkel’s Consumer Brands business offers a diverse portfolio of consumer brands with a focus on Laundry & Home Care and Hair Care. Its consumer brands include Persil, Schwarzkopf, Perwoll, Syoss, Somat and Bref.

Stahl is a manufacturer of specialty coatings for flexible materials headquartered in the Netherlands. With subsidiaries in Europe, Asia, and the Americas, the company is active worldwide. Its portfolio comprises leather finishing coatings, performance coatings, and packaging coatings, serving customers mainly in mobility (automotive and aviation interiors), packaging, fashion & lifestyle, and home furnishing industries.

determination_table_content.details:

its proposed acquisition of all of the share capital in Stahl Parent
B.V. (together with its subsidiaries, Stahl), as described in the
